[fast] During a skill test you are performing, exhaust an [[Ally]] asset you control: For this test, you get +1 skill value, plus an additional +1 for each skill icon that [[Ally]] asset has that matches this test's type.  [elder_sign] effect: +3. Ready an [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Charlie Kane: The Politician
text: Deck Size: 30. Class Choices: At deck creation, choose 2 classes ([guardian], [seeker], [rogue], [mystic], or [survivor]). Deckbuilding Options: [[Ally]] cards level 0-5, Neutral cards level 0-5, cards from your chosen classes level 0-2. Deckbuilding Requirements (do not count toward deck size): Bonnie Walsh, Burden of Leadership, 1 random basic weakness.

name: Bonnie Walsh: Loyal Assistant
text: Charlie Kane deck only.  [reaction] After you exhaust Bonnie Walsh: Ready another [[Ally]] asset you control. (Limit once per round.)

name: Burden of Leadership
text: Revelation - If you control no [[Ally]] assets, shuffle Burden of Leadership back into your deck. Otherwise, for each [[Ally]] asset you control, you must either exhaust it or deal it 1 direct damage and 1 direct horror.

name: Hunter's Armor
text: Customizable.

name: Runic Axe
text: Customizable. Uses (4 charges). Replenish 1 of these charges at the start of each round. [action]: Fight. You get +1 [combat] for this attack. Before this attack, you may spend any number of charges to imbue the axe with that many different inscriptions - - Accuracy - You get an additional +2 [combat] for this attack. - Power - This attack deals +1 damage.

name: Runic Axe
text: Customizable. Uses (4 charges). Replenish 1 of these charges at the start of each round. [action]: Fight. You get +1 [combat] for this attack. Before this attack, you may spend any number of charges to imbue the axe with that many different inscriptions - - Accuracy - You get an additional +2 [combat] for this attack. - Power - This attack deals +1 damage.

name: Custom Modifications
text: Customizable.  Attach to a [[Firearm]] asset you control. Limit 1 per asset.  [reaction] When you reveal a non-[auto_fail] chaos token attacking with attached asset, exhaust Custom Modifications: Cancel that chaos token, return it to the chaos bag, and reveal a new one.

name: Obsidian Bracelet
text: Obsidian Bracelet may only be assigned damage and/or horror dealt by treachery effects.  Obsidian Bracelet may be assigned damage and/or horror dealt to other investigators at your location.

name: Bolas
text: Evade. Attempt to evade using [combat] instead of [agility]. If you succeed and the enemy is non-[[Elite]], attach Bolas to it. Attached enemy gets -1 evade value. Forced - After attached enemy moves: Exhaust it.

name: Breach the Door
text: Test [combat] (1). If you succeed, attach Breach the Door to your location with 1 resource on it for each point you succeeded by, as leads.  Reduce attached location's shroud by 1 for each lead on Breach the Door.

name: Grievous Wound
text: Fast. Play after you successfully attack a non-[[Elite]] enemy using a [[Melee]] asset. Attach Grievous Wound to the attacked enemy. Forced - At the end of the round: Deal 1 damage to attached enemy.

name: Motivational Speech
text: Parley. Choose an investigator at your location. That investigator may play an [[Ally]] asset from their hand, reducing its cost by 3.

name: One in the Chamber
text: Fast. Play at the end of an attack or effect that spends the last ammo from a [[Firearm]] asset you control.  Add 1 ammo to that asset. You get +3 skill value for your next attack with that asset this round.

name: Fighting Lessons
text: Commit only to a skill test during an attack or evasion attempt.  You can commit this skill to an investigator's test at any location.

name: Helping Hand
text: You may commit Helping Hand to any type of test. Max 1 committed per test.  While Helping Hand is committed to a skill test, double the skill icons of each other card committed to that test.

name: Bestow Resolve
text: Uses (4 charges).  [fast] During a skill test performed by an investigator at your location or a connecting location, spend 1 charge: Commit a non-weakness card from your hand to that skill test, treating all of its skill icons as [wild].

name: Field Agent
text: You get +1 [intellect].  [fast] Exhaust Field Agent and deal 1 horror to it: Discover 1 clue at your location.

name: Guard Dog
text: [fast] Exhaust Guard Dog: Engage an enemy at your location. That enemy attacks you. [reaction] When an enemy attack deals damage and/or horror to Guard Dog: Deal 1 damage to the attacking enemy. Card design by the Council-in-Exile at Arkham Nights 2020.

name: Handcuffs
text: Fast. [action] If Handcuffs is not attached to an enemy: Evade. Use only on a [[Humanoid]] enemy. This evasion attempt uses [combat] instead of [agility]. If you succeed, remove all doom from the just-evaded enemy and attach Handcuffs to it. If the attached enemy is non-[[Elite]], it cannot ready and doom cannot be placed on it.

name: Prepared for the Worst
text: Choose an investigator at your location. That investigator search the top 9 cards of their deck for a [[Weapon]] asset, adds it to their hand or plays it (paying its resource cost), and shuffles their deck.

name: Martyr's Vambrace: Remnant of the Unknown
text: You get +1 skill value while resolving revelation abilities on encounter cards.  [reaction] After another investigator at your location draws a non-peril encounter card, before resolving that card's effects, exhaust Martyr's Vambrace: You are considered to have drawn that encounter card, instead.

name: Girish Kadakia: ICPC Punjab Detective
text: Girish Kadakia may be assigned damage and/or horror dealt to other investigators at your location.  [fast] During a skill test being performed by an investigator at your location, exhaust Girish Kadakia: That investigator gets +2 skill value for this test. If this test is successful, heal 1 damage or horror from Girish Kadakia.

name: Ever Vigilant
text: One at a time, investigators at your location, as a group, may play up to 4 assets in total from their hands, reducing the resource cost of each by 1.

name: Alchemical Distillation
text: Customizable. Uses (3 supplies).  [action] Spend 1 supply: Choose an investigator at your location and test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, that investigator performs one of the following options -  - Draw 2 cards.  - Gain 2 resources.

name: Empirical Hypothesis
text: Customizable. Limit 1 per investigator. Forced - At the start of the round: Choose one of the following criteria for this round - You fail a test by 2 or more. - You succeed at a test by 3 or more. When the chosen criteria is met, you may exhaust Empirical Hypothesis to add 1 evidence to it. [fast] Spend 1 evidence: Draw 1 card.

name: Empirical Hypothesis
text: Customizable. Limit 1 per investigator. Forced - At the start of the round: Choose one of the following criteria for this round - You fail a test by 2 or more. - You succeed at a test by 3 or more. When the chosen criteria is met, you may exhaust Empirical Hypothesis to add 1 evidence to it. [fast] Spend 1 evidence: Draw 1 card. (Limit twice per round.)

name: The Raven Quill
text: Customizable. When you purchase The Raven Quill, name a [[Tome]] or [[Spell]] asset and record that name on its upgrade sheet.  Attach to a named asset you control.  [reaction] When you resign or the game ends: Either mark a checkbox on The Raven Quill's upgrade sheet, or reduce the experience cost to upgrade the attached asset before the next scenario by 1.

name: Dissection Tools
text: [reaction] After an enemy at your location is defeated: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on this card, as evidence. While Dissection Tools has... - ... 1 or more evidence, you get +1 [agility]. - ... 2 or more evidence, you get +1 [combat]. - ... 3 or more evidence, you get +1 sanity.

name: Grim Memoir
text: Uses (4 secrets). [action] Spend 1 secret: Investigate. You get +2 [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed by 2 or more, you may draw 1 card.

name: Research Notes
text: [reaction] After a player card ability places 1 or more of your clues on your location: Place that many resources on Research Notes, as evidence.  [action]: Test [intellect] (0). For each point you succeed by, you may spend 1 evidence to discover 1 clue at your location.

name: Research Notes
text: Mutated.  [reaction] After a player card ability places 1 or more of your clues on your location: Place that many resources on Research Notes, as evidence.  [action]: Test [intellect] (0). For each point you succeed by, you may spend 1 evidence to discover 1 clue at your location (max 3 evidence spent per round).

name: Bizarre Diagnosis
text: Place 1 of your clues on your location. Then, heal 3 damage from an investigator or [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Captivating Discovery
text: Search the top 6 cards of your deck. You may place up to 3 of your clues on your location. For each clue placed in this way, choose and add 2 of the searched cards to your hand. Shuffle your deck.

name: Map the Area
text: Investigate. Add your [willpower] or [agility] to your skill value for this investigation. If you succeed, instead of discovering clues at this location, attach Map the Area to it. Limit 1 per location. Reduce the difficulty of all skill tests at attached location by 1.

name: Analysis
text: After revealing a chaos token for this skill test, you may place 1 of your clues on your location to cancel that chaos token, return it to the chaos bag, and reveal a new one. (You may do this any number of times.)

name: Lab Coat
text: [reaction] When you would fail a skill test on a [seeker] card by 1 or less, exhaust Lab Coat: You succeed by 0, instead.

name: Orphic Theory
text: Uses (4 secrets).  [fast] Spend 1 secret: Choose any non-weakness treachery not attached to an [[Elite]] enemy. Until the end of the round, treat that card's printed text box as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]).

name: Existential Riddle
text: Parley. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location and test [willpower] or [intellect] (8). This test gets -1 difficulty for each other card in your hand. If you succeed, automatically evade the chosen enemy and attach Existential Riddle to it. Attached enemy gains aloof.

name: Guidance
text: Choose another investigator at your location who has yet to take their turn this round. During their turn this round, the chosen investigator gets +1 to each of their skills, and may take an additional action. Playing Guidance does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Dr. William T. Maleson: Working on Something Bigger
text: [reaction] When any investigator draws an encounter card from the encounter deck, exhaust Dr. William T. Maleson and place 1 of your clues on your location: That investigator draws another card from the top of the encounter deck, chooses one of those 2 cards to resolve, and cancels and discards the other.

name: Press Pass
text: [reaction] After you spend 1 or more clues or place 1 or more clues on your location, exhaust Press Pass: You may take an additional action during this turn (or your next turn if it is not currently your turn).

name: Surgical Kit
text: Uses (4 supplies).  [reaction] When one of your card effects heals damage from an investigator, spend 1 supply: Either that effect heals 1 additional damage, or you draw 1 card and heal 1 horror.  [action] [action]: Heal 3 damage from an investigator or [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Fingerprint Kit
text: Uses (3 supplies). [action] Exhaust Fingerprint Kit and spend 1 supply: Investigate. You get +2 [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed, you discover 2 additional clues at your location.

name: Gray's Anatomy: The Doctors' Bible
text: [action]: Choose a card at your location and test [intellect] (1). For each point you succeed by, choose one -  - The next time that card would be healed this round, heal +1 damage/horror (to a maximum of +3).  - The next time that card would be dealt damage this round, deal +1 damage (to a maximum of +3).

name: Damning Testimony
text: Customizable. Uses (3 evidence). [action] Exhaust Damning Testimony and choose an enemy at any location: Investigate (your location). If you succeed, you may spend 1 evidence to discover 1 additional clue at the chosen enemy's location.

name: Friends in Low Places
text: Customizable. When you purchase Friends in Low Places, choose and record a [[Trait]] on its upgrade sheet.  Look at the top 6 cards of your deck. For each looked-at card with the chosen [[Trait]](s), you may spend 1 resource to add that card to your hand. Shuffle the remaining cards into your deck.

name: Honed Instinct
text: Customizable. Fast. Play this card only after one of the following conditions is met:  - The agenda or act advances.  - You succeed at a skill test by 3 or more.  Immediately take an action as if it were your turn. (Max once per round.)

name: Disguise
text: Uses (4 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Evade. You get +2 [agility] for this evasion attempt. If you succeed and the evaded enemy is non-[[Elite]], it does not ready during the next upkeep phase.

name: Embezzled Treasure
text: [fast] Exhaust Embezzled Treasure: Move up to 2 resources from your resource pool to Embezzled Treasure (to a maximum of 10 resources on it). Forced - When you resign or the game ends: For every 2 resources on Embezzled Treasure, an investigator of your choice begins the next scenario of the campaign with 1 additional resource in their resource pool.

name: Thieves' Kit
text: Uses (6 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Investigate. You may use [agility] instead of [intellect] for this investigation. If you succeed, gain 1 resource.

name: Hidden Pocket
text: Fast. Play only during your turn.  Attach to a [[Clothing]] or [[Armor]] asset you control.  Attached asset gains: "You have 1 additional hand or accessory slot (your choice), which can only be used to hold an [[Illicit]] asset."

name: Hit and Run
text: Fast. Play only during your turn.  Put an [[Ally]] asset from your hand into play. At the end of your turn, if that asset is still in play, return it to your hand.

name: I'll take that!
text: Fast. Play when you successfully evade a [[Humanoid]] enemy or successfully investigate.  Play an [[Item]] asset from your hand, reducing its cost by X, where X is the amount you succeeded by. Attach "I'll take that!" to that asset. Attached asset gains [[Illicit]].

name: Kicking the Hornet's Nest
text: Search the top 9 cards of the encounter deck for a non-[[Elite]] enemy and spawn it engaged with you (instead of its normal spawn location). Then, discover 1 clue at your location and gain X resources, where X is that enemy's health value. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Quick Getaway
text: Fast. Play when an enemy attacks you. Evade. Attempt to evade the attacking enemy. If you succeed, cancel that enemy's attack.

name: Calculated Risk
text: Max 1 committed per skill test. Commit only to a test you are performing of any type, and only if it is your turn. Calculated Risk gains [wild] for each action you performed this turn (including this one). After this test ends, end your turn.

name: Stylish Coat
text: [reaction] When you gain 1 or more resources during your turn via another player card effect, exhaust Stylish Coat: Gain 1 additional resource.

name: Chuck Fergus: O'Bannion Driver
text: [reaction] When you play a [[Tactic]] or [[Trick]] event, exhaust Chuck Fergus: Choose one -  - That event gains fast.  - That event costs 2 fewer resources to play.  - You get +2 skill value while performing a skill test during the resolution of that event.

name: Chuck Fergus: O'Bannion Driver
text: Mutated. [reaction] When you play a [[Tactic]] or [[Trick]] event, exhaust Chuck Fergus: Choose one -  - That event gains: "Fast. Play only during your turn."  - That event costs 2 fewer resources to play.  - You get +2 skill value while performing a skill test during the resolution of that event.

name: Dirty Fighting
text: Limit 1 per investigator.  While attacking, parleying, or attempting to evade an exhausted enemy, you get +2 skill value.  [reaction] After you evade an enemy, exhaust Dirty Fighting: Take a fight action against that enemy. Ignore the aloof keyword for this attack.

name: Breaking and Entering
text: Investigate. Add your [agility] value to your skill value for this investigation. If you succeed by 1 or more, you may automatically evade an enemy at this location. If you succeed by 3 or more, return Breaking and Entering to your hand at the end of your turn. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Thieves' Kit
text: Uses (6 supplies). [action] Spend 1 supply: Investigate. For this investigation, you may use [agility] instead of [intellect], and you get +1 skill value. If you succeed, gain 1 resource (2 resources instead if you succeed by 2 or more).

name: Trigger Man
text: [reaction] After Trigger Man enters play: Attach an [[Illicit]] asset from your hand to Trigger Man (it is considered to be in play and under your control). [fast] Exhaust Trigger Man and spend 1 resource: Resolve an [action] ability on attached asset, without paying its [action] cost. Resolve that ability with a base skill value of 4.

name: Underworld Market
text: Permanent. Exceptional. You get +10 deck size.  [reaction] Before drawing your opening hand, choose 10 [[Illicit]] cards from your deck: Shuffle them together and place them next to Underworld Market, as your market deck.  [reaction] At the start of your turn: Reveal the top 2 cards of your market deck. You may spend 1 resource to draw 1 of them. Place the rest on the bottom, in any order.

name: Underworld Market
text: Permanent. Exceptional. You get +10 deck size.  [reaction] Before drawing your opening hand, choose 10 [[Illicit]] cards from your deck: Shuffle them together and place them next to Underworld Market, as your market deck.  [reaction] At the start of your turn: Reveal the top 2 cards of your market deck. You may spend 1 resource to draw 1 of them. Place the rest on the bottom, in any order.

name: Clean Sneak
text: Fast. Play only during your turn.  For each exhausted non-swarm enemy at your location, choose a different option:  - Gain 2 resources.  - Deal 2 damage to that enemy.  - Discover 1 clue at your location.  - Draw 1 card.

name: Living Ink
text: Customizable. When you purchase Living Ink, choose a skill and circle it on its upgrade sheet.  Uses (3 charges).  Remove 1 charge from Living Ink at the start of each of your turns. If Living Ink has no charges, discard it.  You get +1 to the chosen skill(s).

name: Summoned Servitor
text: Customizable. As an additional cost to play Summoned Servitor, you must discard another asset you control.  Summoned Servitor enters play at your location. It can take 1 action during each of your turns, which can only be used to take the following action:  - Move Summoned Servitor to a connecting revealed location.

name: Power Word
text: Customizable. Fast. Play only during your turn. Attach to a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. [action] If attached enemy is at your location: Parley. Give it a command (limit once per round per command) - - "Go." This enemy moves once in a direction of your choice. - "Cower." This enemy exhausts.

name: Power Word
text: Mutated. Customizable. Fast. Play only during your turn. Attach to a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. [action] If attached enemy is at your location: Parley. Test [willpower](3). If you succeed, give it a command (limit once per round per command) - "Go." This enemy moves once in a direction of your choice.

name: Power Word
text: Mutated. Customizable. Fast. Play only during your turn. Attach to a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. [action] If attached enemy is at your location: Parley. Test [willpower](2). If you succeed, give it a command (limit once per round per command) - "Go." This enemy moves once in a direction of your choice.

name: Ceremonial Sickle
text: [action]: Fight. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [combat] for this attack. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Ceremonial Sickle and place 1 doom on it to get +1 skill value and deal +1 damage for this attack. - If this attack defeats an enemy, remove 1 doom from Ceremonial Sickle.

name: Dowsing Rod
text: [action]: Investigate. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [intellect] for this investigation. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Dowsing Rod and place 1 doom on it to move to a connecting location. - If this investigation discovers the last clue at a location, remove 1 doom from Dowsing Rod.

name: Hallowed Chalice
text: [action]: Choose an investigator at your location and either (choose one) -  - Exhaust Hallowed Chalice and place 1 doom on it to heal 2 damage or 2 horror from that investigator.  - Heal 1 damage or 1 horror from that investigator. If you heal the last damage or horror from that investigator, remove 1 doom from Hallowed Chalice.

name: Onyx Pentacle
text: [action]: Evade. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [agility] for this evasion attempt. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Onyx Pentacle and place 1 doom on it to target any enemy at your location or a connecting location, and get +1 skill value for this evasion attempt. - If you succeed by 2 or more, remove 1 doom from Onyx Pentacle.

name: Eldritch Initiation
text: For each arcane slot you have, draw 1 card (max 5). For each filled arcane slot you have, choose and discard 1 card from your hand.

name: Explosive Ward
text: Before you play Explosive Ward, you may discard any number of cards from your arcane slots.  Deal X damage to a non-[[Elite]] enemy engaged with you. X cannot be greater than the number of empty arcane slots you have. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: String of Curses
text: Parley. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location and either (choose one): - Automatically evade that enemy and place 1 doom on it. It cannot take damage for the remainder of the round. Discover 1 clue at your location. - If that enemy has 1 or more doom on it, defeat it. Gain 1 resource for each doom that was on it.

name: Binder's Jar: Interdimensional Prison
text: You get +1 arcane slot for each enemy beneath Binder's Jar.  [reaction] After a non-[[Elite]] enemy is defeated at your location: Place that enemy facedown beneath Binder's Jar (limit 2 enemies beneath it).  [reaction] When an enemy attacks you, discard an enemy beneath Binder's Jar that shares a [[Trait]] with the attacking enemy: Cancel that attack.

name: Ghastly Possession
text: After you commit Ghastly Possession to a skill test on an asset, choose one:  - Place 1 doom on that asset. Ghastly Possession gains [wild][wild].  - If this test is successful, either remove 1 doom from this asset, or replenish half of its uses (rounded down).

name: Astral Mirror
text: For each of your arcane slots that are empty, you have one additional hand slot.  You may take an additional action during your turn, which can only be used to play an asset into one of your hand slots.

name: Elle Rubash: Purifying Purpose
text: You get +1 skill value while resolving a skill test on an attached asset.  1 doom on each attached asset does not count towards the agenda's doom threshold.  [fast] Exhaust Elle Rubash: Choose an asset in your play area with 1 or more doom on it. Attach it to Elle Rubash, or switch it with an attached asset. (Limit 2 attached assets).

name: Moonlight Ritual
text: Fast. Play only during your turn.  Remove all doom from a non-[[Elite]] card at your location.

name: Sin-Eater
text: Permanent. Exceptional.  [fast] Exhaust Sin-Eater: Move 1 doom from an asset you control to Sin-Eater. Then, either ready that asset or place 1 charge on it.  [action]: Remove all doom from Sin-Eater.

name: Uncage the Soul
text: You may discard a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] asset you control. Play a [[Spell]] or [[Ritual]] card from your hand or discard pile, reducing its resource cost by 3.

name: Ceremonial Sickle
text: [action]: Fight. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [combat] for this attack. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Ceremonial Sickle and place 1 doom on it to get +3 skill value and deal +1 damage for this attack. - If this attack defeats an enemy, ready Ceremonial Sickle and remove all doom from it.

name: Dowsing Rod
text: [action]: Investigate. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [intellect] for this investigation. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Dowsing Rod and place 1 doom on it to move to a connecting location and get +2 skill value for this investigation. - If this investigation discovers the last clue at a location, ready Dowsing Rod and remove all doom from it.

name: Onyx Pentacle
text: [action]: Evade. Either use your [willpower] or get +1 [agility] for this evasion attempt. When you initiate this ability, choose one - - Exhaust Onyx Pentacle and place 1 doom on it to target any enemy at your location or a connecting location, and get +3 skill value for this evasion attempt. - If you succeed by 2 or more, ready Onyx Pentacle and remove all doom from it.

name: Pocket Multi Tool
text: Customizable. Limit 1 per investigator.  [fast] During a skill test you are performing, exhaust Pocket Multi Tool: You get +1 skill value for this test.

name: Makeshift Trap
text: Customizable. Uses (2 time). If Makeshift Trap has no time, discard it. Attach to your location. Each non-[[Elite]] enemy at attached location get -1 fight and -1 evade. Forced - At the end of the round: Remove 1 time from Makeshift Trap.

name: Grizzled
text: Customizable. When you purchase Grizzled, choose and record two [[Traits]] on its upgrade sheet. If this is a skill test on or against an encounter card (including fighting, evading, or parleying), Grizzled gains [wild][wild] for each of the chosen traits that encounter card possesses.

name: Idol of Xanatos: Watcher Beyond Time
text: [reaction] When you are dealt damage and/or horror, exhaust Idol of Xanatos and discard up to 3 cards from your hand: Cancel that much damage/horror.

name: Improvised Shield
text: Improvised Shield can only be played from your discard pile. Forced - When Improvised Shield is defeated: Shuffle it into your deck instead of discarding it.

name: End of the Road
text: Fast. Play during your turn only if the final agenda is in play.  Draw 1 card, gain 1 resource, and gain 1 additional action. Remove End of the Road from the game.

name: Exploit Weakness
text: Fast. Play before revealing a chaos token during an attack or evasion attempt you are performing, and only if the difficulty of this test is currently 0.  This test automatically succeeds. Instead of this test's normal effects, discard the attacked or evaded enemy (if it is [[Elite]], automatically evade it and deal it 3 damage instead).

name: Making Preparations
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Choose two skills ([willpower], [intellect], [combat], or [agility]). Until the end of the investigation phase, each investigator gets +1 to each of the chosen skills, and -1 to each other skill.

name: Predator or Prey
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - If there are no enemies in play, draw 1 card. Otherwise, you must decide (choose one): - Each unengaged non-[[Elite]] enemy moves once toward the nearest investigator. - Each investigator disengages from each enemy engaged with them and moves once away from the nearest enemy.

name: Shed a Light
text: Fast. Play before revealing a chaos token during an investigation you are performing, and only if the difficulty of this test is currently 0.  This test automatically succeeds. You discover 1 additional clue at this location and 1 additional clue at any location.

name: Shed a Light
text: Fast. Play before revealing a chaos token during an investigation you are performing, and only if the difficulty of this test is currently 0.  This test automatically succeeds. You discover 1 additional clue at this location and 1 additional clue at any location.

name: At a Crossroads
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Choose an investigator and decide (choose one): - The chosen investigator must immediately take an action as if it were their turn, then discards 1 card at random from their hand. - The chosen investigator loses 1 action during their next turn, then draws 3 cards.

name: Lifeline
text: Max 1 per turn.  Fast. Play when an investigator's turn would end.  That investigator may take X additional actions during their turn. X is the number of skill tests they failed during their turn. Exile Lifeline.

name: Nature of the Beast
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Reveal the top 3 cards of the encounter deck. Choose an investigator to draw 1 of the revealed cards and discard the rest. Then, choose an investigator to discover 1 clue at any revealed location.

name: Gumption
text: Max 1 committed per skill test.  You may commit Gumption to any type of skill test.  While Gumption is committed to a skill test, that test gets -2 difficulty.

name: Baseball Bat
text: [action]: Fight. You get +2 [combat] for this attack. This attack deals +1 damage. If a [skull] or [auto_fail] symbol is revealed during this attack, either (choose one): - Return Baseball Bat to your hand after this attack. - This attack deals an additional +1 damage. Discard Baseball Bat after this attack.

name: Katja Eastbank: Keeper of Esoteric Lore
text: [reaction] When you draw a non-weakness card from the top of your deck, before resolving any of its effects, exhaust Katja Eastbank: Place that card facedown beneath her (limit 5 cards beneath her). Draw a new card to replace it.  [action]: Draw any card beneath Katja Eastbank.

name: Heed the Dream
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - Reveal the top card from each investigator's deck and place them in a pile. Repeat this two more times. Choose one of the three piles. Each investigator draws each of their cards from that pile. Shuffle each weakness in each other pile into its owner's deck. Remove each other revealed card from the game.

name: Salvage
text: Choose an [[Item]] asset in your discard pile. Either (choose one): - Remove that asset from the game and gain resources equal to its cost. - Play that asset (paying its cost).

name: Old Keyring
text: Uses (3 keys). If there are no keys on Old Keyring, discard it. [action]: Investigate. Your location gets -2 shroud for this investigation. If you succeed, remove 1 key from Old Keyring, and if this test's difficulty is 0, you discover 1 additional clue.

name: Old Keyring
text: Uses (3 keys). If there are no keys on Old Keyring, discard it. [action]: Investigate. Your location gets -2 shroud for this investigation. If you succeed, remove 1 key from Old Keyring, and if this test's difficulty is 0, you discover 1 additional clue.

name: Fickle Fortune
text: Max 2 [[Dilemma]] per round. Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the current agenda. Each investigator heals 3 damage and 3 horror. - Remove 1 doom from the current agenda. Each investigator takes 1 direct damage and 1 direct horror. Remove Fickle Fortune from the game.

name: Hyperphysical Shotcaster: Theoretical Device
text: Customizable. Uses (4 aether). If Hyperphysical Shotcaster has no aether, discard it. [action] Spend 1 aether: Resolve the Manifest ability of the Hyperphysical Shotcaster's current form. [fast] Exhaust Hyperphysical Shotcaster: Change its form (to one you have unlocked on its upgrade sheet).

name: Tool Belt
text: Each attached asset takes up no slots and its text box is treated as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]).  [fast] Exhaust Tool Belt: Choose one - attach a [[Tool]] asset in your play area to Tool Belt, switch a [[Tool]] asset in your play area with an attached asset, or detach an attached asset.

name: Refine
text: As an additional cost to play Refine, spend an action.  Immediately mark a checkbox on an upgrade sheet for a customizable card you own, even if it is not in play.  Max once per game for each investigator.

name: Flashlight
text: Uses (4 supplies). [reaction] When you perform a skill test while investigating or attempting to evade, spend 1 supply: This test gets -2 difficulty. Card design by the Council-in-Exile at Arkham Nights 2020.

name: Soul Sanctification
text: Permanent. Exceptional.  For every point of damage and/or horror you heal in excess of an investigator's current damage/horror, place 1 resource on Soul Sanctification, as an offering. This still counts as healing.  [fast] Spend 1 offering: You get +2 skill value for this test. (Limit twice per test.)

name: Lurker in the Dark
text: [guardian] investigator only. Hunter. Spawn - Any connecting location. Lurker in the Dark can only be attacked or damaged using [[Weapon]] assets or [[Tactic]] events, and takes 1 fewer damage from each source.

name: Quantum Paradox
text: [seeker] investigator only. As an additional cost to play Quantum Paradox, you must choose and discard 4 other cards from your hand. Forced - If Quantum Paradox is in your hand at the end of your turn: Reveal it and take 1 horror.

name: Pay Your Due
text: [rogue] investigator only. As an additional cost to play Pay Your Due, you may spend any number of additional actions. Reduce its cost by 5 for each additional action spent. Forced - If Pay Your Due is in your hand at the end of your turn: Reveal it and take 1 damage.

name: Ectoplasmic Horror
text: [mystic] investigator only.  Hunter.  The first time you reveal a chaos token while attacking or attempting to evade Ectoplasmic Horror, reveal an additional token for each empty arcane slot you have.

name: Underprepared
text: [survivor] investigator only.  Play only if Underprepared is the only card in your hand and you have exactly 1 resource in your resource pool.  While Underprepared is in your hand, each card you commit to skill tests is considered to have 1 fewer matching skill icon.

name: Riddles and Rain
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -1 (-3 instead if you have 2 or more clues).  [tablet]: -1. If there is a concealed mini-card at your location, reveal another chaos token.  [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, you must either spend 1 clue or place 1 doom on an enemy in the shadows.

name: Riddles and Rain
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -2 (-4 instead if you have 2 or more clues).  [tablet]: -2. If there is a concealed mini-card at your location, reveal another chaos token.  [elder_thing]: -4. If you fail, you must either spend 1 clue or place 1 doom on an enemy in the shadows.

name: When It Rains...
text: Forced - When this agenda advances: Move all doom on it to the next agenda.

name: Just Out of Reach
text: Each investigator takes 1 horror. Put the set-aside Kensington Gardens, Westminster Abbey, and Big Ben locations into play. The lead investigator draws the set-aside The Red-Gloved Man enemy and resolves his concealed keyword. (Spawn The Red-Gloved Man in the shadows. Take his mini-card and 1 [per_investigator] decoys, shuffle them facedown, and put them into play divided as evenly as possible among the locations nearest to the lead investigator.) Each investigator loses each of their clues. Advance the act directly to act 2a (do not resolve act 1b).

name: Figures in the Fog
text: Forced - When this agenda advances: Move all doom on it to the next agenda.

name: He Got Away!
text: Mark 1 time in your Campaign Log. Set The Red-Gloved Man enemy and each concealed mini-card aside, out of play. Put the set-aside Tower Bridge and Tower of London locations into play. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the set-aside Crimson Conspiracy and Outsiders encounter sets into the encounter deck. Each investigator loses each of their clues. Advance the act directly to act 3a (do not resolve act 2b).

name: Mysterious Shapes
text: The lead investigator must decide (choose one):  - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Coterie Agent enemy and draw it. Shuffle the encounter deck.  - Each investigator takes 1 damage and 1 horror.

name: The Plot Succeeds
text:
If it is act 1, 2, or 3:
Despite your best efforts, the trail has gone cold. You cannot find any sign of the man with the red gloves or his cohorts. What's worse, you have no idea what their goal was in London to begin with. You have no choice but to report back to Inspector Flint with what little you have learned.
(→R4) (page 8) If it is act 4:
Your search for the man with the red gloves has led you into a hidden section of the tower prison. You duck under stone archways and sneak through dank, forgotten halls until finally, you find him: the man with the red gloves. He has lifted the lid from a stone crypt and is studying the corpse inside. The moment he sees you enter, he pulls a small ruby marble from the eyesocket of the unfortunate soul, then slips back into the shadows.
(→R3) (page 8)

name: Clues and Capers
text: Objective - As a group, spend the requisite number of clues to advance before the agenda advances.

name: Movement in the Dark
text: Put the set-aside Kensington Gardens, Westminster Abbey, and Big Ben locations into play. The lead investigator draws the set-aside The Red-Gloved Man enemy and resolves his concealed keyword. (Spawn The Red-Gloved Man in the shadows. Take his mini-card and 1 [per_investigator] decoys, shuffle them facedown, and put them into play divided as evenly as possible among the locations nearest to the lead investigator.) Advance the agenda directly to agenda 2a (do not resolve agenda 1b).

name: The Game is Afoot!
text: Objective - Chase down the mysterious figure before the agenda advances. If an investigator engages The Red-Gloved Man, advance.

name: The Chase is On!
text: Set The Red-Gloved Man aside, out of play. Put the set-aside Tower Bridge and Tower of London locations into play. Shuffle the set-aside Crimson Conspiracy and Outsiders encounter sets into the encounter deck, along with the encounter discard pile. Advance the agenda directly to agenda 3a (do not resolve agenda 2b).

name: Eyes in the Tower
text: Objective - Only investigators at the Tower of London may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: He's Here!
text: Put the set-aside Tower Prison location into play. Attach the set-aside The Eye of Ravens key to the Tower Prison, not controlled by anyone.  Move all concealed mini-cards in play to the Tower Prison.  Draw the set-aside The Red-Gloved Man enemy and resolve his concealed keyword, putting all of those concealed mini-cards into play at the Tower Prison.

name: Caught Red-Handed
text: The Red-Gloved Man gains: "[action]: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (5) to interrogate The Red-Gloved Man. If you succeed, place 1 resource on him." Objective - Stop The Red-Gloved Man from stealing The Eye of Ravens. If he is defeated, or if there are 1 [per_investigator] resources on him, advance.

name: The Plot is Foiled
text:
If you defeated The Red-Gloved Man:
The figure reels backward, grasping at his wound. Something small clatters to the ground. The man says nothing - only glares at you from the darkness. His gloves seem to ripple around his hands, like the disturbed surface of a deep crimson pool, and after a moment, he straightens himself. Then, blending into the shadows, he tips his hat and is gone.
Add The Red-Gloved Man to the victory display. (→R1) (page 7) If there are 1 [per_investigator] resources on The Red-Gloved Man:
The man stops and faces you, seemingly intrigued by your line of questioning, although in the darkness you cannot study his expression. "... interesting," he whispers. "Very interesting." His voice has a strange, uneven quality, like he is testing the tone of each word. Like one unaccustomed to speaking.
Add The Red-Gloved Man to the victory display. (→R2) (page 7)

name: Rainy London Streets
text: Rainy London Streets gets +X shroud. X is the current act number. Forced - If there are no clues on Rainy London Streets: Add clues to it until it has 1 [per_investigator] clues on it. [action]: Resign. "We should wait for Inspector Flint..."

name: Big Ben
text: [fast]: Choose a concealed mini-card at Big Ben or a connecting location and test [agility] (2). If you succeed, look at the revealed side of that concealed mini-card (without exposing it). If you fail, take 1 horror. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Westminster Abbey
text: [action]: Parley. Choose a location and test [willpower] (1) to ask around the abbey. For each point you succeed by, look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at the chosen location (without exposing it). (Group limit once per game.)

name: Kensington Gardens
text: [reaction] After you expose a decoy at Kensington Gardens: Heal 1 horror. Forced - After you expose an enemy at Kensington Gardens: Take 1 horror.

name: The Tower Bridge
text: [action]: "Maybe there is another way in..." Test [willpower] or [intellect] (5). If you succeed, put the set-aside Traitors' Gate location into play.

name: Traitors' Gate
text: [action]: Test [combat] or [agility] (5). If you succeed, reveal the Tower of London. If you fail, take 1 damage.

name: Tower of London
text: Forced - After you end your turn at the Tower of London: You must either lose 2 resources, or each enemy in the shadows attacks you.

name: Tower of London
text: The main gate into the Tower is locked and securely guarded. As an additional cost to enter the Tower of London, investigators at The Tower Bridge must spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group.

name: Tower Prison
text: [fast] While an investigator at Tower Prison is performing a skill test, spend 1 clue: That investigator gets +2 skill value for this test. Forced - When a concealed mini-card would enter play anywhere other than Tower Prison: Put it into play at Tower Prison, instead.

name: Tower Prison
text: Forced - When a concealed mini-card would enter play anywhere other than Tower Prison: Put it into play at Tower Prison, instead.

name: The Red-Gloved Man: Shrouded in Mystery
text: Concealed 1 [per_investigator]. Retaliate.

name: The Eye of Ravens: Macabre Ruby Marble
text: Shift - During a skill test at your location, the performing investigator sets their base skill value for this test to 6. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Eye of Ravens: Macabre Ruby Marble
text: Shift - Draw the top card of the encounter deck. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: Dead Heat
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -1 for every 1 [per_investigator] civilians slain.  [cultist]: -4. If you fail, choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. That enemy readies and attacks you.  [tablet]: -1. You may take 1 damage. If you do not, a civilian at your location is slain.  [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, choose and discard 1 card from your hand.

name: Dead Heat
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -2 for every 1 [per_investigator] civilians slain.  [cultist]: -6. Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location. That enemy readies and attacks you.  [tablet]: -2. Take 1 damage. A civilian at your location is slain.  [elder_thing]: -4. If you fail, discard 1 card from your hand at random.

name: Gnashing Teeth
text: Each [[Risen]] enemy and each [[Ghoul]] enemy gains "Spawn - Random location."

name: The Risen Dead
text: For every 1 [per_investigator] civilians slain, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Risen]] or [[Ghoul]] enemy and spawn it at a random location. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.  Each investigator decides (choose one):  - Heal 2 damage from each [[Elite]] enemy in play.  - 1 civilian at your location is slain.

name: Empty Streets
text: Each [[Risen]] enemy and each [[Ghoul]] enemy gains hunter and "Spawn - Random location."

name: The Hungering Dead
text: Each investigator suffers 1 physical trauma. Check which enemies are in play. - If Razin Farhi is in play, proceed to (→R1) (page 13). - Otherwise, proceed to (→R2) (page 13).

name: Find Amaranth
text: Objective - At the end of the round, investigators at a [[Ritual Site]] location may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.

name: Night Has Come
text: Spawn the set-aside Amaranth enemy at Bahia Palace Gardens, (Lurking Corruption) side faceup, and attach the set-aside The Last Blossom key to her. Put the set-aside Save the Civilians story card into play next to the agenda deck. Shuffle the set-aside Khalid enemy into the encounter deck.

name: Ritual of Life and Death
text: Forced - After a civilian is slain, if 4[per_investigator] or more civilians have been slain: Advance this act. Objective - If Amaranth would be defeated, advance.

name: The Land is Dark
text:
If this act advanced because of its forced ability:
"Razin, my love!" exclaims Amaranth. The body in the ritual circle rises to stand, along with two newly-resurrected warriors. Amaranth embraces the ancient philosopher, the two of them entwined with awful black tendrils. She speaks softly: "Let me make this place a paradise for you, my love. A true paradise."
Spawn the set-aside Razin Farhi enemy at Bahia Palace Gardens. Shuffle both copies of the set-aside Ancient Raider enemy into the encounter deck.
If this act advanced because Amaranth would be defeated:
Your final blow knocks Amaranth back, toppling an ancient brazier. As hungry flames consume the remnants of the ritual and the corpses, she screeches in anguish. "Do you have any idea what you have done? Razin, my love..."
Flip Amaranth to her (Corruption Revealed) side, keeping all tokens and attachments on her, and move her to a random location with no investigators. Shuffle both copies of the set-aside Ancient Raider enemy into the encounter deck.

name: Queen of Nothing at All
text: Objective - If neither Amaranth nor Razin Farhi are in play, advance.

name: Stand By Me
text: Check your Campaign Log. - If the cell knows Amaranth's real name, proceed to (→R3) (page 13). - Otherwise, proceed to (→R4) (page 13).

name: Marrakesh Railway Station
text: [fast]: Move (to a connecting location). (Group limit once per turn.) [action]: Resign. You flee the city. (Note: If there are no investigators in play, all remaining civilians in play will be slain.) Forced - If Marrakesh Railway Station has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Marrakesh Railway Station
text: Forced - If Marrakesh Railway Station has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Jemaa el-Fnaa Square
text: [action] If 1 or more civilians are on this location: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. You may play an asset from among those cards (paying its cost). Shuffle the rest into your deck. (Limit once per game.) Forced - If Jemaa el-Fnaa Square has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Jemaa el-Fnaa Square
text: Forced - If Jemaa el-Fnaa Square has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Saadian's Tombs
text: Saadian's Tombs gets +1 shroud for each civilian on it. Forced - When a non-[[Elite]] enemy enters or spawns at Saadian's Tombs, before it engages any investigators: Exhaust it. Forced - If Saadian's Tombs has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Saadian's Tombs
text: Forced - If Saadian's Tombs has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Tanneries
text: [fast] Choose a [[Risen]] enemy from the encounter discard pile and spawn it engaged with you: Place clues on Tanneries (from the token pool) equal to that enemy's health. Forced - If Tanneries has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Tanneries
text: Forced - If Tanneries has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Bahia Palace Gardens
text: [reaction] After you rescue a civilian on Bahia Palace Gardens: Heal 1 damage or 1 horror. Forced - If Bahia Palace Gardens has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Bahia Palace Gardens
text: Forced - If Bahia Palace Gardens has no civilians on it: Swap it with its set-aside [[Abandoned]] version.

name: Marrakesh Railway Station
text: As an additional cost for you to move out of Marrakesh Railway Station, you must spend 1 resource. [action]: Resign. You run for your life. (Note: If there are no investigators in play, all remaining civilians in play will be slain.)

name: Jemaa el-Fnaa Square
text: While you are at Jemaa el-Fnaa Square, increase the cost of each asset you play by 2.

name: Saadian's Tombs
text: Forced - When a non-[[Elite]] enemy enters Saadian's Tombs, before it engages any investigators: Heal all damage from it.

name: Tanneries
text: Each [[Risen]] enemy at Tanneries gets +1 fight and +1 evade.

name: Bahia Palace Gardens
text: Forced - When you end your turn at Bahia Palace Gardens: Take 1 damage or 1 horror.

name: Save the Civilians
text: The civilians of Marrakesh are under attack! By getting them out of harm's way, you may be able to avert a massacre. (Note: Civilians still on locations are neither rescued nor slain.) [action] Spend 1 clue: Rescue a civilian from your location (place it on this card). Forced - When the game ends: flip this card (keep all tokens on it).

name: Aftermath
text: Compare the number of rescued civilians on Aftermath with the number of slain civilians on the Scenario Reference card. (Note: Civilians still on locations are neither rescued nor slain.) - If the number of rescued civilians is more than the number of slain civilians, each investigator earns 2 bonus experience. If the number of rescued civilians is more than twice the number of slain civilians, each investigator earns 2 additional bonus experience. - If the number of slain civilians is more than the number of rescued civilians, each investigator suffers 1 mental trauma. If the number of is more than twice the number of rescued civilians, each investigator suffers 1 additional mental trauma. - If it is a tie, nothing happens.

name: Amaranth: Lurking Corruption
text: Hunter. Forced - When damage or horror from Amaranth's attack is assigned to an asset: Defeat that asset and Amaranth shifts each of her attached keys.

name: Amaranth: Corruption Revealed
text: Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - When Amaranth attacks: She shifts each of her attached keys. If damage and/or horror from this attack is assigned to an asset, defeat that asset.

name: Razin Farhi: Reanimated Artificer
text: Alert. Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - After any key is shifted: If Razin Farhi is exhausted, ready him. Otherwise, he makes an immediate attack against the engaged investigator.

name: Thrall
text: The first copy of Thrall drawn each round gains surge.  [fast] During your turn: Attempt to evade Thrall. It gains alert for this evasion attempt. (Limit once per turn).

name: Ancient Raider
text: Hunter. Retaliate. Forced - After Ancient Raider attacks you: 1 civilian at your location is slain.

name: Khalid: Beloved Companion
text: Hunter. Forced - After Khalid enters play: Move each other non-[[Elite]] enemy and civilian from its location to a connecting location (distributed as evenly as possible).

name: Famine
text: Revelation - Put Famine into play next to the agenda deck. Increase the cost of each card you play by 1. Forced - At the end of the round: Discard Famine from play.

name: Cornered!
text: Revelation - Test [combat] (3). If you fail, either (choose one): - 1 civilian at your location is slain. - Take 1 damage for each point you fail by.

name: The Last Blossom: Paracausal Symbiote
text: Shift - Heal 1 damage and 1 horror from each investigator at your location. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Last Blossom: Paracausal Symbiote
text: Shift - Heal 1 damage from each enemy in play. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: Sanguine Shadows
text: Easy / Standard [skull]: -X. X is the number of targets on this card. [cultist]: -5. If you fail by 2 or more, shuffle each concealed mini-card at your location and at each connecting location facedown, then place them at random in the positions they were in. [tablet]: -1. Cancel the effects and icons of each card committed to this skill test. [elder_thing]: -2. If you fail, you must either place 1 doom on the current agenda, or the nearest [[Coterie]] enemy attacks you (even if it is in the shadows).

name: Sanguine Shadows
text: Hard / Expert [skull]: -X. X is 1 more than the number of targets on this card. [cultist]: -7. If you fail by 2 or more, shuffle each concealed mini-card at your location and at each connecting location facedown, then place them at random in the positions they were in. [tablet]: -2. Cancel the effects and icons of each card committed to this skill test. [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, you must either place 1 doom on the current agenda, or the nearest [[Coterie]] enemy attacks you (even if it is in the shadows).

name: Where is She...?
text: Forced - At the start of the game, or after the agenda deck resets: Set the amount of doom on this agenda to 1 [per_investigator]. Forced - After you expose a decoy: La Chica Roja attacks you. (Hint - when this agenda advances, La Chica Roja will collect one of the pieces she seeks!)

name: Bamboozled!
text: Reveal each concealed mini-card in play and set each decoy aside, out of play. Find the location with a target on it nearest to La Chica Roja (or her mini-card). La Chica Roja collects that target (place it on her). If there are 3 targets on La Chica Roja, proceed to (→R3). Otherwise, resolve La Chica Roja's concealed keyword, distributing each of those concealed mini-cards as evenly as possible among each location with a target. Reset the act and agenda decks to agenda 1 and act 1. Add clues to each location until it has clues equal to its clue value.

name: The Scarlet Shadow
text: Objective - Expose the thief's whereabouts. If an investigator engages La Chica Roja, advance.

name: Exposed!
text:
If this is the first time this act has been advanced:
Following all of your leads, you manage to beat La Chica Roja to her target and lie in ambush. She arrives soon after and sets to work, but it's not long before she realizes something is wrong. She hums a jazzy improvisation and saunters around the room, scanning for something. "Come on out," she says at last, a smirk on her lips. You reveal yourself and tell her to stop what she is doing. "Or what?" she grins. "You gonna make me?"
Otherwise:
Once again, you beat your rival to the punch, and she spots you immediately. "You again?" she says dismissively. "Don't you have anything better to do? I'm quite busy, as you can see."

name: In the Searchlight
text: La Chica Roja gains: "[action] Parley. Test [intellect] or [agility] (6) to outsmart the thief. You may spend 1 clue to reduce this test's difficulty by 2. If you fail, La Chica Roja attacks you." Objective - If La Chica Roja would be defeated, instead advance. Objective - If an investigator successfully parleys with La Chica Roja, advance.

name: The Getaway
text:
Find the location with a target on it nearest to La Chica Roja. Place that target on the scenario reference card. Then, if there are 3 targets on the scenario reference card, skip the rest of this act and instead proceed to Scenario Interlude: Cast a Light. Otherwise, continue reading the following.
"Fine, you can have this one," the mysterious thief calls to you as she makes her escape. "There's more where that came from, anyway!" Before you can catch her, with a twirl of her stark red coat, she vanishes into the shadows without a trace.
Heal all damage from La Chica Roja, return her to the shadows, and resolve her concealed keyword, distributing each of those concealed mini-cards as evenly as possible among each location with a target. Reset the act and agenda decks to agenda 1 and act 1. Each investigator loses each of their clues. Add clues to each location until it has clues equal to its clue value.

name: Avenida de Mayo
text: If your difficulty level is... - ...Easy, X is 2. - ...Standard, X is 3. - ...Hard, X is 4. - ...Expert, X is 5. [action]: Resign. Looks like La Chica Roja wins this game.

name: Casa Rosada
text: [action] Spend 1 clue: Test [intellect] (4) to observe the staff. You may spend 1 additional clue to reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at this location (without exposing it). For every 2 points you succeed by, look at an additional mini-card at this location.

name: Catedral Metropolitana
text: [action] Spend 1 clue: Test [agility] (4) to watch the rooftops of the cathedral. You may spend 1 additional clue to reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at this location (without exposing it). For every 2 points you succeed by, look at an additional mini-card at this location.

name: Cementario de la Recoleta
text: [action] Spend X clues and take X horror: Look at the revealed side of X concealed mini-cards at this location (without exposing them).

name: Palacio Errázuriz
text: [action] Spend 1 clue: Test [combat] (4) to break into the mansion. You may spend 1 additional clue to reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at this location (without exposing it). For every 2 points you succeed by, look at an additional mini-card at this location.

name: The Cabildo
text: [fast]: Look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at this location (without exposing it). Then, draw the top card of the encounter deck. You may spend 2 clues to cancel this card's effects and discard it.

name: Banco de la Provincia
text: [action] Spend X clues and thrice as many resources: Look at the revealed side of X concealed mini-cards at this location (without exposing them).

name: Teatro Colón
text: [action] Spend 1 clue: Test [willpower] (4) to stake out the rafters of the opera house. You may spend 1 additional clue to reduce the difficulty of this test by 2. If you succeed, look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at this location (without exposing it). For every 2 points you succeed by, look at an additional mini-card at this location.

name: La Chica Roja: The Girl in the Carmine Coat
text: Concealed 5. Forced - When an investigator engaged with La Chica Roja moves: Disengage her from that investigator.

name: La Chica Roja: Your Watchful Shadow
text: [reaction] When your turn ends, exhaust La Chica Roja: Place your investigator mini-card in the shadows. While you are in the shadows, you are not at any location. Enemies that would spawn engaged with you instead spawn at your previous location. At the start of your turn, move back to your previous location (or any revealed location if you cannot).

name: Bound Nightgaunt
text: Hunter.  While attacking Bound Nightgaunt, double the negative modifier of each revealed chaos token.

name: Cat and Mouse
text: Peril. Surge. Revelation - If there is a concealed mini-card at your location, you may expose it. If an enemy's mini-card would be exposed by this effect, instead flip it back over and shuffle it facedown with the concealed mini-cards at your location.

name: Calling Card
text: Revelation - Attach to a location without a target, and without a copy of Calling Card attached. While Calling Card is in play, clues cannot be spent (at any location). [reaction] After you successfully investigate attached location by 2 or more: Discard Calling Card. Look at the revealed side of a concealed mini-card at any location (without exposing it).

name: Outsmarted
text: Revelation - Test [intellect] (4). If you fail, you must decide (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the current agenda. This effect can cause the agenda to advance. - The nearest [[Coterie]] enemy attacks you.

name: Seeing Red
text: Forced - After a decoy is exposed: Place 1 doom on this agenda. Forced - When this agenda would advance by reaching its doom threshold: Each investigator is defeated and suffers 1 mental trauma. Objective - If the Sanguine Watcher is defeated, advance.

name: The Sanguine Watcher: With the Ruby Spectacles
text: Retaliate. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if The Sanguine Watcher is ready: Each investigator must either take 2 damage or 2 horror.

name: Apportioned Ka
text: Concealed 4. Forced - After Apportioned Ka is exposed: Deal 5 damage to the Sanguine Watcher. Return Apportioned Ka to the shadows and resolve its concealed keyword, distributing those concealed mini-cards as evenly as possible among each non-[[Central]] location without a target.

name: The Weeping Lady: Broken Statuette
text: Shift - Discover 1 clue at your location or a connecting location. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Weeping Lady: Broken Statuette
text: Shift - Each investigator must discard an asset they control, if able. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: Dealings in the Dark
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -X. X is the highest number of clues on a [[Cultist]] enemy in play, to a maximum of 3. (If it is act 3, X is 3.)  [cultist]: -5. You may place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy to change this token's modifier to -1.  [tablet]: -3. If you fail, place 1 of your clues on your location.  [elder_thing]: -2. If you fail, place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.

name: Dealings in the Dark
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -X. X is the total number of clues on [[Cultist]] enemies in play, to a maximum of 4. (If it is act 3, X is 4.)  [cultist]: -7. You may place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy to change this token's modifier to -3.  [tablet]: -4. If you fail, place 1 of your clues on your location.  [elder_thing]: -2. Place 1 doom on the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.

name: Agents of the Dark
text: Each non-weakness [[Cultist]] enemy gains concealed 1. Forced - After you evade a [[Cultist]] enemy, return it to the shadows and resolve its concealed keyword: Take control of 1 of its clues.

name: In a Shadow of Voidlight
text: In player order, each investigator must choose: Either place 1 clue (from the token pool) on Clues Unveiled, or search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Cultist]] enemy and draw it; it gains concealed 1 (if no [[Cultist]] enemy is found, place 1 clue on Clues Unveiled, instead). Shuffle the encounter deck if it is searched. For each eclipse that was on this agenda when it advanced, an investigator must resolve the above effect an additional time. Flip this agenda back over, adding 1 resource to it, as an eclipse. (It keeps each other eclipse it had, as well.)

name: Search for the Manuscript
text: Objective - Find the talisman before the cult does! If either the cult, or investigators at any location(s), possess the requisite number of clues as a group, advance. (Do not spend any clues when you advance.)

name: Manuscript Discovered
text:
If the investigators reached the clue threshold first:
You discover what you believe to be the talisman's resting place and scramble to find the Key before your adversaries find you. But to your surprise, inside is only an ancient manuscript.
If the cult reached the clue threshold first:
You discover what you believe to be the talisman's resting place and find it already torn apart. Resting on the ground nearby is a torn-up manuscript, which you hastily begin to restore.
In either case: Resolve the concealed keyword on each non-weakness [[Cultist]] enemy not already in the shadows (as granted by agenda 1a). Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the set-aside Umbral Harbinger and Emissary from Yuggoth enemies into the encounter deck. Proceed to Act 2 Setup in the Campaign Guide.

name: Search for the Talisman
text: Objective - Find the talisman before the cult does! If either the cult, or investigators at the location designated as the east gate, possess the requisite number of clues as a group, advance. (Do not spend any clues when you advance.)

name: Talisman Discovered
text: If the investigators reached the clue threshold first, attach The Twisted Antiprism key to any investigator at the east gate.  If the cult reached the clue threshold first, attach The Twisted Antiprism key to the [[Cultist]] enemy with the most clues.  Reveal each concealed mini-card in play. For each enemy's mini-card, move that enemy from the shadows to that location. Set each concealed mini-card aside, out of play.  Put the locations from act 1 that were previously set aside back into play, with Hagia Sophia to the left of the entrance to the Grand Bazaar. These two locations are connected to one another for the rest of the scenario. Put Galata Docks into play.  If the cult reached the clue threshold first, for every 1 [per_investigator] more clues they had than the investigators, move the [[Cultist]] enemy with The Twisted Antiprism once toward Galata Docks.  Each investigator and [[Cultist]] enemy loses each of their clues. Flip The Unveiling story card over to its other side, removing all clues from it. Advance the act and agenda deck directly to "The Chase." It is both the current act and the current agenda.

name: The Chase
text: Objective - "Get this thing out of here!" If an investigator with The Twisted Antiprism is at Galata Docks, you may proceed to (→R1) (page 25). Objective - "Don't let them get away with the Key!" If an enemy with The Twisted Antiprism is at Galata Docks, or if the investigator with The Twisted Antiprism is defeated, proceed to (→R6) (page 25).

name: Rush for the Key
text: Each enemy in play moves and attacks as if it were the enemy phase (without exhausting). If there are fewer enemies in play than the number of investigators, in player order, discard cards from the top of the encounter deck until a [[Cultist]] enemy is discarded, and draw it (shuffling the encounter discard pile to form a new encounter deck if necessary). Repeat this until there are enemies in play equal to the number of investigators. Flip this card back over.

name: The Unveiling
text: Forced - After 1 or more doom is placed on a [[Cultist]] enemy: Flip them to their clue side. If an enemy has more than 3 clues on it, move the clues in excess of 3 to Clues Unveiled, below. The number of clues possessed by the cult is equal to the total number of clues on [[Cultist]] enemies in play plus the number of clues on Clues Unveiled, below.

name: The Unsealing
text: Each non-weakness enemy gains: "Hunter. Prey - Investigator with The Twisted Antiprism." If The Twisted Antiprism is attached to that enemy, it instead gains "Patrol (Galata Docks)." Forced - When 1 or more doom would be placed on an enemy: It instead moves and attacks as if it were the enemy phase (without exhausting). Forced - After a non-weakness enemy attacks the investigator with The Twisted Antiprism: Attach The Twisted Antiprism to that enemy, [[Unstable]] side faceup. Then, that enemy shifts it. [reaction] After you evade or defeat the enemy with The Twisted Antiprism: Take control of The Twisted Antiprism, attaching it to your investigator.

name: Galata Docks
text: You can only enter Galata Docks from Galata. As an additional cost for you to do so, you must discard any combination of cards from your hand or assets from your play area with at least 3 matching skill icons.

name: Galata Docks
text: You can only enter Galata Docks from Galata. As an additional cost for you to do so, you must discard any combination of cards from your hand or assets from your play area with at least 3 matching skill icons.

name: Galata
text: [fast] Spend 3 resources: Parley. Expose a concealed mini-card at any location. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Obelisk of Theodosius
text: Forced - After you fail a skill test while investigating the Obelisk of Theodosius: Move 1 of your clues to the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.

name: Istanbul University
text: [action]: Test [willpower] or [intellect] (6). If you succeed, take control of 1 clue on Clues Unveiled. (Max one success per game.)

name: Hagia Sophia
text: Hagia Sophia gets +3 shroud for each study token on it. [action]: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues on Hagia Sophia (from the token pool). Place 1 resource on Hagia Sophia (also from the token pool), as a study token.

name: Grand Bazaar: Darkened Alley
text: Increase the difficulty to fight or evade each concealed mini-card at this location by 2.

name: Grand Bazaar: Public Baths
text: While you are at this location, treat the text box of each [[Weapon]] asset you control as if it were blank (except for [[Traits]]).

name: Grand Bazaar: Marble Fountain
text: [fast]: Parley. Take control of 1 clue from a [[Cultist]] enemy at this location. Then, return that enemy to the shadows and resolve its concealed keyword. (Limit once per round.)

name: Grand Bazaar: Crowded Shops
text: Forced - After you expose a decoy at this location: Move 1 clue from this location to the nearest [[Cultist]] enemy.

name: Grand Bazaar: Busy Walkway
text: Forced - When you would move out of this location: Test [agility] (3). If you fail, you must either lose 1 action or cancel the effects of the move.

name: Grand Bazaar: Rooftop Access
text: [reaction] After you move to this location from a connecting location: Test [agility] (4). If you succeed, move again. Forced - After an enemy moves to this location via hunter or patrol: Resolve that keyword again.

name: Grand Bazaar: Jewelers' Road
text: [action]: Investigate. If you succeed, instead of discovering clues, you may play a [[Charm]] or [[Relic]] asset from your hand, reducing its cost by 1 for each point you succeeded by (to a minimum of 0).

name: Ece Şahin: The Vermillion-Veiled Lady
text: [fast] Exhaust Ece Şahin: Look at the top card of any investigator's deck. If it is not a weakness, you may place it facedown beneath Ece Sahin (limit 5 cards beneath her). Forced - After any key is shifted: The owner of each card beneath Ece Şahin draws that card.

name: Umbral Harbinger
text: Hunter. Forced - When Umbral Harbinger is dealt damage: Place 1 doom on the [[Cultist]] enemy nearest to it.

name: Sinister Aspirant (A)
text: Alert. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Sinister Aspirant is ready: Place 1 doom on it.

name: Sinister Aspirant (B)
text: Alert. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Sinister Aspirant is ready: Place 1 doom on it.

name: Sinister Aspirant (C)
text: Alert. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Sinister Aspirant is ready: Place 1 doom on it.

name: Shadowed
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, take 1 horror and Shadowed gains surge. Otherwise, find the [[Cultist]] enemy nearest to you. Place 1 doom on that enemy and test [willpower] (X), where X is that enemy's fight value. If you fail, take 2 horror.

name: Accosted
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Cultist]] enemies in play, take 1 damage and Accosted gains surge. Otherwise, find the [[Cultist]] enemy nearest to you. Place 1 doom on that enemy and test [agility] (X), where X is that enemy's evade value. If you fail, take 2 damage.

name: Light Out of Void
text: Peril. Revelation - You must either (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the [[Cultist]] enemy in play with the highest evade. - Take 1 damage and 1 horror. If the investigators possess more clues than the cult, or if it is act 3, resolve this revelation ability a second time.

name: The Twisted Antiprism: Talisman of Infinite Darkness
text: Shift - Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. Draw 1 of those cards and shuffle the rest back into your deck. Flip this key to its [[Unstable]] side.

name: The Twisted Antiprism: Talisman of Infinite Darkness
text: Shift - The nearest investigator must place 1 doom on an asset they control, an encounter card at their location, or the current agenda. Then, if an investigator shifted this key, flip it to its [[Stable]] side.

name: Dancing Mad
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -X. X is the number of enemies in the shadows (max 3).  [cultist]: -4. If you fail, put a decoy into play at your location, flip it over, and shuffle each concealed mini-card at your location facedown.  [tablet]: -3. If you fail, set each non-weakness card committed to this test aside, out of play, as a hollow.  [elder_thing]: -1. If there is a concealed mini-card at your location, either take 1 damage or treat this token's modifier as -3.

name: Dancing Mad
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -X. X is the number of enemies in the shadows (max 5).  [cultist]: -6. If you fail, put a decoy into play at your location, flip it over, and shuffle each concealed mini-card at your location facedown.  [tablet]: -3. Set each non-weakness card committed to this test aside, out of play, as a hollow.  [elder_thing]: -1. If there is a concealed mini-card at your location, either take 2 damage or treat this token's modifier as -5.

name: Silence Speaks
text: Forced - If there are 5 [per_investigator] or more set-aside hollows: Choose and remove 3 [per_investigator] of them from the game and place 1 doom on this agenda. This effect may cause this agenda to advance.

name: Vanishing Sky
text: The lead investigator must decide (choose one): - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy with the concealed keyword and draw it (resolving its concealed keyword). Shuffle the encounter deck. - Reveal the top card of each investigator's deck. Each investigator draws each weakness they revealed in this way. Set each other revealed card aside, out of play, as a hollow.

name: Easy Prey
text: Forced - If there are 4 [per_investigator] or more set-aside hollows: Choose and remove 2 [per_investigator] of them from the game and place 1 doom on this agenda. This effect may cause this agenda to advance.

name: Mimicry
text: The lead investigator must decide (choose one): - Search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy with the concealed keyword and draw it (resolving its concealed keyword). Shuffle the encounter deck. - Reveal the top card of each investigator's deck. Each investigator draws each weakness they revealed in this way. Set each other revealed card aside, out of play, as a hollow.

name: A Betrayal of Eyes
text: Forced - If there are 3 [per_investigator] or more set-aside hollows: Choose and remove 1 [per_investigator] of them from the game and place 1 doom on this agenda. This effect may cause this agenda to advance.

name: Erasure
text: Remove each hollow from the game. Gather each hollow that was removed from the game throughout this scenario. Each investigator finds their hollow with the highest experience cost and exiles it. In the Campaign Log, record erased from existence: and next to this, record the names of each unique card exiled in this way. For the rest of the campaign, players cannot include any copies of those cards (by title) in their decks (immediately remove any copies from each investigator's deck). (→R2) (page 31)

name: False Step (v. I)
text: [action]: Resign. "The deal's off!" You leave Desi and his crew to their fate. Forced - If Desiderio Delgado Álvarez leaves play: Set him aside, out of play. Each investigator takes 1 horror.

name: Pursued into the Streets
text: If the Desiderio Delgado Álvarez story asset is in play, set him aside, out of play (removing all tokens and attachments from him). Find the set-aside Desiderio Delgado Álvarez enemy with the encounter card back. The lead investigator draws that enemy, resolving his concealed keyword. One at a time, in player order, each other investigator chooses a different set-aside [[Coterie]] enemy and draws it, resolving its concealed keyword. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the remainder of the Crimson Conspiracy and Cleanup Crew encounter sets into the encounter deck.

name: False Colors (v. I)
text: [reaction] When an investigator would resolve an enemy's concealed X keyword, spend X clues, as a group: Spawn it engaged with that investigator instead. [action]: Resign. You leave Desi and his crew to their fate. Objective - If there are no enemies in the shadows, investigators may spend the requisite clues to advance.

name: Truths Untold
text: Ready the Desiderio Delgado Álvarez enemy, remove all tokens from him, and discard all attachments from him. Find the previously set-aside Desiderio Delgado Álvarez story asset, flip him to his enemy side, and spawn him at the same location as the one in play, randomizing them so you do not know which is which.

name: False Step (v. II)
text: Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Deal X total damage to investigators at Café Luna, divided as they wish. X is the number of enemies in the shadows. [reaction] After a [[Coterie]] enemy takes any amount of damage: Discover half that many clues at Café Luna (rounded down).

name: Pursued into the Streets
text: Shuffle the encounter discard pile and each set-aside [[Outsider]] enemy into the encounter deck.  If Desiderio Delgado Álvarez is not in the shadows, return him to the shadows and resolve his concealed keyword.  Put each set-aside [[Havana]] location into play.  Take each concealed mini-card in play, shuffle them, and randomly distribute them as evenly as possible among each location other than Café Luna.

name: False Colors (v. II)
text: [action] As a group, investigators at your location place clues they control on a [[Coterie]] enemy at your location equal to that enemy's remaining health: Parley. You gain some insight into the situation. Remove all doom from that enemy and automatically evade it. [action]: Resign. "I thought we had a deal!" You get out of the city while you still can. Objective - Discover why your contacts are after you. If there are 4 [per_investigator] total clues among enemies in play, advance.

name: Truths Untold
text: Reveal each concealed mini-card in play. For each enemy's mini-card, move that enemy from the shadows to that location. Then, set each concealed mini-card aside, out of play.  Ready the Desiderio Delgado Álvarez enemy, remove all tokens from him, and discard all attachments from him. Spawn the other set-aside Desiderio Delgado Álvarez enemy at the same location as the one in play, randomizing them so you do not know which is which.  Remove all clues from each enemy in play. For each clue removed from an enemy in this way, heal 1 damage from that enemy.

name: False Light
text: Each copy of Desiderio Delgado Álvarez loses concealed X, loses hunter, gains aloof, and gains the following ability: "[action]: Parley. Check the Campaign Log. If the cell knows Desi's past, you ask a question only he can answer. Look at the other side of this enemy." Objective - If either copy of Desiderio Delgado Álvarez is defeated, advance. (Do not look at its other side.)

name: No Man Like Me
text: For the remainder of the campaign, do not look at the other sides of either copy of Desiderio Delgado Álvarez until you are instructed to do so. Find the copy of Desiderio Delgado Álvarez that is in play (if both copies are in the victory display, return one to play). Store this copy of Desiderio Delgado Álvarez in a safe place where you may refer to it later in the campaign. (→R1) (page 31)

name: Café Luna: Bastion of Remembrance
text: Café Luna gets -1 shroud for each [[Outsider]] enemy in play. Forced - If there are no clues on Café Luna: Add clues to it until it has 1 [per_investigator] clues on it.

name: El Malecón
text: [reaction] After you expose a decoy at El Malecón: Expose another concealed mini-card at El Malecón. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Jardines de la Tropical
