Shuffle each of the set-aside Mi-Go Drone enemies into the encounter deck, along with the encounter discard pile.
Regardless of how many times this act has advanced, read the following:
As the hours pass, the ooze seeps back into the ground and lies dormant once more.
Place 1 [per_investigator] clues on each revealed [[Oozified]] location, to a maximum of its clue value. The lead investigator chooses a random set-aside story card and draws it, resolving the side with the [[Part 1]] trait. Reset the act deck to act 1a.

name: The Crater
text: [action] Spend 1 countermeasure: Deal 3 damage to Subject 8L-08 or a [[Manifold]] enemy at any location. Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator at The Crater takes 1 damage.

name: Research Site
text: [action] Spend 2 clues: Gain 1 countermeasure. [action] Spend 1 countermeasure: Place 2 clues on Research Site (from the token pool).

name: Temporary HQ
text: [action] Spend 1 countermeasure: Choose one -  - Heal 3 damage.  - Heal 3 horror.  - Gain 5 resources.  - Draw 3 cards.

name: Fungus Mound
text: [action] Spend 1 countermeasure: Choose a [[Mi-Go]] enemy at any location and test [intellect] (0). For each point you succeed by, deal 1 damage to the chosen enemy (to a maximum of 5).

name: Sewer
text: [action]: Test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, move to a location up to X connections away from Sewer, where X is the amount you succeeded by.

name: Bridge
text: [fast]: Choose an exhausted [[Manifold]] enemy at Bridge and test [intellect] (X), where X is that enemy's evade value. If you succeed, defeat the chosen enemy. (Max one success per game.)

name: Water Tower
text: [action] If there are no clues on Water Tower: Test [intellect] (3) to find the structure's weakness. If you succeed, deal 3 damage to each [[Ooze]] enemy at Water Tower and at each connecting location. (Max one success per game.)

name: Church
text: [action] If there are no clues on Church: Choose an [[Ooze]] enemy at Church, or a connecting location. Move the chosen enemy one location away from Church and automatically evade it. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Oozy Lakebed
text: Forced - After a [[Manifold]] enemy spawns at Oozy Lakebed: Place 1 clue on Oozy Lakebed. That enemy cannot take damage this round.

name: Slimy Streets
text: Forced - After you discover any number of clues from Slimy Streets: Reveal that many random tokens from the chaos bag. For each [skull] or [auto_fail] token revealed by this effect, take 1 damage or 1 horror.

name: Desiccated Farmland
text: While you are at Desiccated Farmland, you cannot gain resources. Forced - After you end your turn at Desiccated Farmland: Lose 1 resource.

name: Rescue the Chemist
text: Revelation - Spawn the set-aside Mi-Go Abductor at the Fungus Mound. Put the set-aside University Chemist asset into play at the location farthest from all investigators. Put this card into play, next to the act deck. Forced - After an investigator who controls University Chemist enters the Temporary HQ: Remember that "the chemist was saved." Flip this card over. Forced - If University Chemist is defeated or devoured: Remember that "the formula was not completed." Flip this card over.

name: Rescue the Chemist
text:
Read only if "the formula was not completed:" Remove Mi-Go Abductor, University Chemist, and this card from the game. Each investigator takes 1 horror. Lose 1 countermeasure.
Read only if "the chemist was saved:"
The chemist thanks you for aiding her and hands you the concoction her team has been working on - a universal solvent which can dissolve almost anything.
Put the set-aside Universal Solvent story asset into play under any investigator's control. Remove University Chemist from the game. Add Mi-Go Abductor and this card to the victory display. Deal 3 damage to any [[Ooze]] enemy in play.

name: Recover the Sample
text: Revelation - Spawn the set-aside Mi-Go Harvester at the Fungus Mound. Put the set-aside Meteorite Sample asset into play at The Crater. Put this card into play, next to the act deck. Forced - After an investigator who controls a Meteorite Sample enters the Research Site: Remember that "the sample was recovered." Flip this card over. Forced - If Meteorite Sample is attached to the Mi-Go Harvester and it is at the Fungus Mound: Remember that "the sample was lost." Flip this card over.

name: Recover the Sample
text:
Read only if "the sample was lost:" Remove Mi-Go Harvester, Meteorite Sample, and this card from the game. Lose 2 countermeasures.
Read only if "the sample was recovered:"
The few researchers who remain subject the meteorite shard to all kinds of tests. But before they can reach any sort of conclusion, a second creature breaks free from within. Panic erupts throughout the research station, but as the creature does not immediately attack, you wait and observe it closely. It begins to follow you everywhere you go, crawling along the ground like a lost duckling.
Put the set-aside Pet Oozeling story asset into play under any investigator's control. Remove Meteorite Sample from the game. Add Mi-Go Harvester and this card to the victory display. Gain 2 countermeasures.

name: Drive Off the Mi-Go
text: Revelation - Spawn the set-aside Mi-Go General at the Fungus Mound. Place 1 [per_investigator] clues on the Fungus Mound. Put this card into play, next to the act deck. Forced - If Mi-Go General is defeated, or if there are no clues on the Fungus Mound: Remember that "the Mi-Go were driven off." Flip this card over. Forced - If there are 4 [per_investigator] or more clues on Fungus Mound: Remember that "the secret of the ooze was stolen." Flip this card over.

name: Drive Off the Mi-Go
text:
Read only if "the secret of the ooze was stolen:" Remove Mi-Go General and this card from the game. Until the end of the game, each Mi-Go Drone gets +2 health and +2 evade. Place this card next to the scenario reference card, as a reminder.
Read only if "the Mi-Go were driven off:"
Chittering unnaturally, the alien general flees. Its strange weapon unhinges from its arm and clatters to the ground in front of you as it flies into space.
Put the set-aside Mi-Go Weapon story asset into play under any investigator's control. Add Mi-Go General and this card to the victory display. Search the encounter deck, discard pile, and all play areas for each copy of Mi-Go Drone and remove them from the game. Gain 1 countermeasure.

name: Defuse the Explosives
text: Revelation - Spawn the set-aside Mi-Go Meddler at the Fungus Mound. Put this card and the set-aside The Military's Plan asset into play next to the act deck, with 5 damage tokens on it. Place 1 horror token on 1 [per_investigator] different [[Oozified]] locations farthest from the Crater. Horror tokens on locations represent explosives. Forced - If there are no explosives among locations in play: Remember that "the explosives were defused." Flip this card over. Forced - If The Military's Plan has no damage on it: Remember that "the explosives were detonated." Flip this card over.

name: Defuse the Explosives
text:
Read only if "the explosives were detonated:" Remove Mi-Go Meddler, The Military's Plan, and this card from the game. Remove each explosives from each location. Each investigator and [[Ally]] asset at one of those locations takes 3 direct damage. Heal 5 damage from Subject 8L-08.
Read only if "the explosives were defused:"n
"Thanks for helping me," the officer says once the job is done. "I'll make sure our remaining forces know that their conventional tactics are useless against the creature. Perhaps there are other ways we can assist?"
Put the set-aside Lt. Wilson Stewart story asset into play under any investigator's control. Remove The Military's Plan from the game. Add Mi-Go Meddler and this card to the victory display. Each investigator heals 1 horror. Gain 1 countermeasure.

name: University Chemist
text: [action]: Parley. Test [willpower] or [intellect] (3). If you succeed, place 1 resource (from the token pool) on University Chemist. Then, if there are 1 [per_investigator] resources on it, take control of it.

name: Meteorite Sample
text: If uncontrolled and unattached, attach Meteorite Sample to its location. If attached to a location with no clues on it, Meteorite Sample gains: "[action] Test [willpower] or [agility] (6). If you succeed, detach Meteorite Sample and take control of it."

name: Mi-Go General
text: Hunter. Forced - After Mi-Go General attacks you: Move 1 of your clues to Fungus Mound.

name: The Military's Plan
text: Horror tokens on locations represent explosives. Each location with explosives gains: "[action]: Test [intellect] or [agility] (3). If you succeed, remove the horror token on this location." Forced - At the end of the round: Remove 1 damage from this card.

name: Universal Solvent
text: Uses (4 supplies).  [action] Spend 1 supply: Test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, discard an [[Obstacle]] treachery at your location.  [action] Spend 1 supply: Choose a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location and test [intellect] (X), where X is 1 more than that enemy's remaining health. If you succeed, that enemy is defeated. Does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

name: Pet Oozeling
text: [fast] Defeat a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location with 2 or less remaining health. Then, heal all damage from Pet Oozeling, place 1 resource on it, and reveal X chaos tokens, where X is the number of resources on it. If an [auto_fail] symbol is revealed, you are defeated and suffer 1 physical trauma.

name: Mi-Go Weapon
text: Uses (3 ammo). [action] Spend 1 ammo: Fight. The device fires a blast of entropic energy. You get +2 [combat] and deal +1 damage for this attack. If you succeed and the enemy is not defeated, move it one location away from you. (If you succeed by 3 or more, deal +1 damage and move the enemy an additional location away.)

name: Lt. Wilson Stewart: Logistical Genius
text: [fast] Exhaust Lt. Wilson Stewart: Discard a card from your hand or from the top of your deck. Then, for each...  ...[willpower] icon on that card, get +1 for your next test this round.  ...[intellect] icon on that card, draw 1 card.  ...[combat] icon on that card, heal 1 damage or horror from him.  ...[agility] icon on that card, gain 1 resource.

name: Mi-Go Drone
text: Spawn - Location with the most clues (other than Fungus Mound). Surge. Forced - At the start of the enemy phase, if Mi-Go Drone is ready: Move 1 clue from Mi-Go Drone's location to Fungus Mound.

name: Mi-Go Harvester
text: While Mi-Go Harvester is ready, it gains: "Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: If Meteorite Sample is attached to Mi-Go Harvester, move it once toward the Fungus Mound. Otherwise, move Mi-Go Harvester once toward Meteorite Sample's location. Then, if it is at the same location as Meteorite Sample, attach Meteorite Sample to it."

name: Mi-Go Meddler
text: While Mi-Go Meddler is ready, it gains: "Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: If Mi-Go Meddler is at a location with explosives, remove 1 damage from The Military's Plan. Otherwise, move Mi-Go Meddler once toward a location with explosives."

name: Mi-Go Abductor
text: While Mi-Go Abductor is ready, it gains: "Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: If Mi-Go Abductor is at University Chemist's location, deal 1 direct damage to University Chemist. Otherwise, move Mi-Go Abductor once toward University Chemist's location."

name: Subject 8L-08: Extraspacial Anomaly
text: *Subject 8L-08 has a global health pool (see page 6 of the rules for details). If Subject 8L-08 is defeated, proceed to (→R2).

name: Subject 8L-08: Extraspacial Anomaly
text: Subject 8L-08 gets +15 health. If Subject 8L-08 is defeated, proceed to (→R2).

name: Oozeling
text: Spawn - Any empty [[Oozified]] location. Blob 1.

name: Grasping Ooze
text: Spawn - Nearest [[Oozified]] location. Hunter. Forced - After you perform an attack against Grasping Ooze using a [[Melee]] card: Subject 8L-08 devours that card. Blob 3.

name: Cubic Ooze
text: Revelation - Immediately attempt to evade Cubic Ooze (without spending an action). If you fail, spawn Cubic Ooze engaged with you, and it attacks you. If you succeed, spawn Cubic Ooze at your location, exhausted and unengaged. Blob 2.

name: Oozewraith
text: Spawn - Farthest [[Oozified]] location. Alert. Hunter. Retaliate. Blob 5.

name: Vulnerable Heart
text: Massive. Retaliate.  X is the amount of damage on Vulnerable Heart.  Vulnerable Heart cannot be evaded and cannot make attacks of opportunity.  Vulnerable Heart cannot have more than 15 damage on it. All excess damage is canceled.

name: Reality Acid
text: Revelation - Subject 8L-08 devours a random aspect of reality. Reveal two random chaos tokens from the chaos bag and consult pages 16-19 of the rules to determine what it devoured. (If the combination of chaos tokens revealed is not listed, or if the listed aspect cannot be devoured, reveal two new chaos tokens and consult the chart again.)

name: Devouring Ooze
text: Revelation - Test [agility] (4). If you fail, choose an [[Ally]] or [[Item]] asset you control. Subject 8L-08 devours that asset. (If you cannot, take 2 damage instead.)

name: Corrosive Slime
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (4). For each point you fail by, Subject 8L-08 devours either one of your resources or a random card from your hand.

name: Consuming Maw
text: Revelation - Test [agility] (3). Take 1 damage for each point you fail by. Subject 8L-08 devours each card defeated by this effect.

name: Wave of Ooze
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). Take 1 horror for each point you fail by. Subject 8L-08 devours each card defeated by this effect.

name: Caustic Dissemination
text: Revelation - Put Caustic Dissemination into play next to the agenda deck. Forced - At the end of the round: Each investigator at an [[Oozified]] location takes 1 damage and 1 horror. Subject 8L-08 devours each card defeated by this effect. Discard Caustic Dissemination. (Max once per round.)

name: Sticky Feet
text: Revelation - Attach to the nearest [[Oozified]] location. As an additional cost to enter or leave attached location, an investigator must spend an action. [action] Spend 1 countermeasure: Discard Sticky Feet.

name: Replication
text: Revelation - Find the [[Manifold]] enemy with the most damage on it. Discard cards from the top of the encounter deck until another [[Manifold]] enemy with equal or lower printed health is discarded. Spawn that enemy at the same location, then distribute the damage that is on both enemies so that the remaining health on both enemies is as equal as possible. If no enemy is spawned by this effect, Replication gains surge.

name: It's got me!
text: Revelation - Put "It's got me!" into play in your threat area. Forced - At the end of the round: Place 1 resource on "It's got me!" Then, if there are 6 or more resources on it, Subject 8L-08 devours you. [action] If you are at the Research Site, spend 1 countermeasure: Discard "It's got me!"

name: Alien Food Chain
text: Revelation - If there are no [[Manifold]] enemies in play, Alien Food Chain gains surge. Otherwise, attach to the [[Manifold]] enemy with the least remaining health and fully heal that enemy. Attached enemy gets +1 fight, +1 evade, and gains hunter.

name: War of the Outer Gods
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -1 for each clue you have.  [cultist]: -3. If you fail, place 1 resource on the nearest [[Mutated]] enemy, as a mutation.  [tablet]: -3. If you fail, draw the top [[Hex]] card in the encounter discard pile.  [elder_thing]: -4. If you fail, place one card committed to this test facedown underneath an [[Insect]] enemy, as a swarm card.

name: War of the Outer Gods
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -2 for each clue you have.  [cultist]: -4. Place 1 resource on each [[Mutated]] enemy, as a mutation.  [tablet]: -4. Draw the top [[Hex]] card in the encounter discard pile.  [elder_thing]: -5. Place each card committed to this test facedown underneath an [[Insect]] enemy, as a swarm card.

name: The Summoning of Silenus
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next blue agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next blue agenda.

name: Reinforcements
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [tablet] token to the chaos bag.  Shuffle the 2 set-aside Ethereal Entity enemies, the 3 Inevitable End treacheries, and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 1b, spawn the set-aside Hune-Stitched Herald enemy at the Athenaeum of the Empty Sky.

name: The Summoning Progresses
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next blue agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next blue agenda.

name: Summoning of the Servitor
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [tablet] token to the chaos bag.  If the Hune-Stitched Herald enemy is set-aside, shuffle it into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 2b, spawn the set-aside The Inescapable Maw enemy at the Athenaeum of the Empty Sky.

name: The Summoning Nears Completion
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the set-aside Silenus Descends agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the set-aside Silenus Descends agenda.

name: War is Over
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [tablet] token to the chaos bag. Flip Hub Dimension to its (Gateway to Destruction) side. If Hub Dimension is not in play, put it into play, (Gateway to Destruction) side faceup. Spawn the set-aside Silenus enemy at Hub Dimension. Search the encounter deck, encounter discard pile, and each play area for all green and red encounter cards and remove them from the game. Shuffle the encounter deck. Remove each agenda and act from the game and put the set-aside Silenus Descends agenda into play.

name: The Incubation of the Egg
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next green agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next green agenda.

name: Reinforcements
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [cultist] token to the chaos bag.  Shuffle the 2 set-aside Bringer of Paradise enemies, the 3 Hunt Down treacheries, and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 1b, spawn the set-aside Horrific Shoggoth enemy at the Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at.

name: The Incubation Progresses
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next green agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next green agenda.

name: Summoning of the Servitor
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [cultist] token to the chaos bag.  If the Horrific Shoggoth enemy is set-aside, shuffle it into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 2b, spawn the set-aside Vile Broodmaster enemy at the Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at.

name: The Incubation Nears Completion
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the set-aside The Egg Hatches agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the set-aside The Egg Hatches agenda.

name: War is Over
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [cultist] token to the chaos bag. Flip Hub Dimension to its (Gateway to Destruction) side. If Hub Dimension is not in play, put it into play, (Gateway to Destruction) side faceup. Spawn the set-aside Magh'an Ark'at enemy at Hub Dimension. Search the encounter deck, encounter discard pile, and each play area for all blue and red encounter cards and remove them from the game. Shuffle the encounter deck. Remove each agenda and act from the game and put the set-aside The Egg Hatches agenda into play.

name: The Proliferation of the Swarm
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next red agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next red agenda.

name: Reinforcements
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [elder_thing] token to the chaos bag.  Shuffle 3 of the 4 set-aside Trylogog enemies, the 2 Transmogrify treacheries, and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 1b, spawn the remaining set-aside Trylogog enemy. That enemy gains an additional swarming 2.

name: The Proliferation Progresses
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the next red agenda. - If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the next red agenda.

name: The Swarm Spreads
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [elder_thing] token to the chaos bag.  If a copy of Trylogog is set-aside, shuffle it into the encounter deck.  If this is the first agenda to advance to 2b, spawn the set-aside Droning Horde enemy at the Burning Pit.

name: The Proliferation Nears Completion
text: *This agenda has a global doom threshold of 6 per group. Forced - When this agenda advances: - If you are playing in Single Group Mode, remove 6 doom from it and move all excess doom to the set-aside Ezel-zen-rezl Emerges agenda. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, move all doom and wards from it to the set-aside Ezel-zen-rezl Emerges agenda.

name: War is Over
text: If you are playing in Standalone Mode, add 1 [elder_thing] token to the chaos bag. Flip Hub Dimension to its (Gateway to Destruction) side. If Hub Dimension is not in play, put it into play, (Gateway to Destruction) side faceup. Spawn the set-aside Ezel-zen-rezl enemy at Hub Dimension. Search the encounter deck, encounter discard pile, and each play area for all blue and green encounter cards and remove them from the game. Shuffle the encounter deck. Remove each agenda and act from the game and put the set-aside Ezel-zen-rezl Emerges agenda into play.

name: War of the Outer Gods
text: Forced - When any amount of doom is placed on a player card: Place that amount of doom on any agenda as well. Objective - Investigators may only spend the requisite number of clues if no [[Cultist]] enemies are in play.

name: A Portal Opens
text: Put the set-aside Hub Dimension location into play, (Unsealed) side faceup.

name: Close the Portal
text: [action] If you are at a [[Ritual Site]]: You try to seal off the portal. Place 1 of your clues around Hub Dimension. Then you may test [willpower] (2) or [intellect] (2). If you succeed, place 1 of your clues around Hub Dimension. Forced - When any amount of doom is placed on a player card: Place that amount of doom on any agenda as well. Objective - If your group has "sealed off the Hub Dimension," advance.

name: Sealed Off
text:
If you are playing in Single Group Mode:
When your work is done, you and your companions lie on the cold ground, your skin tingling with otherworldly currents. The night sky is filled with stars and no longer blotted out by swirling black entities or alternate dimensions swathed in uncanny mist.
The investigators have sealed Earth away from the cataclysmic war of the Outer Gods. (→R1)
If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode:
This entrance to the hub is sealed, but there are many more entrances that must be sealed before the war can truly end...
Place 2 wards on each agenda. Advance to act 3a.

name: Close All Portals
text: [action] If you are at a [[Ritual Site]]: You try to seal off another portal. Test [willpower] (3) or [intellect] (3). If you succeed, place 1 of your clues around another group's Hub Dimension. Forced - When any amount of doom is placed on a player card: Place that amount of doom on any agenda as well. Objective - If each group has "sealed off the Hub Dimension," advance.

name: The World is Saved
text: The investigators have sealed Earth away from the cataclysmic war of the Outer Gods. (→R1)

name: Arkham
text: Arkham cannot have attachments.  [fast] Spend 1 [per_investigator] resources: Move to a connecting location.

name: Streets of Providence
text: [action] [action]: Deal 3 damage to a non-[[Ancient One]] enemy at a [[Providence]] location in any group. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Athenaeum of the Empty Sky
text: [fast] Draw the top card of the encounter deck: Place 1 ward on the blue agenda. (Group limit once per round.)

name: The Arcade
text: [action]: Test [agility] (4). If you succeed, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Streets of Montréal
text: [action] [action]: Deal 3 damage to a non-[[Ancient One]] enemy at a [[Montréal]] location in any group. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Chateau Ramezay
text: [action]: Test [willpower] (4). If you succeed, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at
text: [fast] Draw the top card of the encounter deck: Place 1 ward on the green agenda. (Group limit once per round.)

name: Streets of New York City
text: [action] [action]: Deal 3 damage to a non-[[Ancient One]] enemy at a [[New York City]] location in any group. (Group limit once per game.)

name: The Burning Pit
text: [fast] Draw the top card of the encounter deck: Place 1 ward on the red agenda. (Group limit once per round.)

name: The Penthouse
text: [action]: Test [combat] (4). If you succeed, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Hub Dimension: Gateway to Destruction
text: Hub Dimension cannot have attachments.  Hub Dimension is connected to each [[Ritual Site]], and vice versa.  [action]: Test [willpower] (2) or [intellect] (2). If you succeed, spend 3 clues or remove 2 clues from around Hub Dimension to deal 2 damage to an [[Ancient One]] enemy (if you succeed by 3 or more, instead deal 3 damage).

name: Hub Dimension: Unsealed
text: While there are 6 [per_investigator] or more clues around Hub Dimension, your group has "sealed off the Hub Dimension."

name: Nihilistic Stargazer
text: Aloof. Warring. Spawn - Athenaeum of the Empty Sky. Forced - At the end of the round, if no green or red enemies are in play: Place 1 doom on the blue agenda.

name: Death and Decay
text: Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - You and each asset you control take 1 direct damage. - Choose an [[Item]] asset you control and remove it from the game.

name: Zealot of Paradise
text: Aloof. Warring. Spawn - Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at. Forced - At the end of the round, if no blue or red enemies are in play: Place 1 doom on the green agenda.

name: Predator's Call
text: Revelation - Attach Predator's Call to the nearest green enemy. If that enemy is [[Mutated]], place 1 resource on it, as a mutation. If no green enemies are in play, Predator's Call gains surge. Attached enemy gets +1 fight, +1 evade, +1 damage, and +1 horror.

name: Disciple of the Swarm
text: Aloof. Warring. Spawn - The Burning Pit. Forced - At the end of the round, if no blue or green enemies are in play: Place 1 doom on the red agenda.

name: Feast of Locusts
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (2). Increase the difficulty of this test by 1 for each swarm card in play. If you fail, take 2 horror (if you fail by 4 or more, instead take 3 horror).

name: Hellfire
text: Revelation - Test [agility] (4). If you fail, either take 2 damage or place 2 of your clues on your location (if you fail and have 4 or more clues, do both instead).

name: Ravages of War
text: Revelation - Each non-[[Elite]] enemy moves and attacks as if it were the enemy phase. Enemies do not exhaust after attacking in this way. If no attacks were made as a result of this effect, Ravages of War gains surge.

name: While They Sleep
text: Revelation - For each faction that has the most enemies in play, place 1 doom on that faction's agenda. If no enemies are in play, place no doom and While They Sleep gains surge.

name: Silenus Descends
text: Each blue enemy loses aloof, loses warring, and gains hunter. Objective - If Silenus is defeated, (→R3).

name: Silenus: The Empty Sky
text: *Silenus has a global health pool of 8[per_investigator]. Hunter. Massive. Forced - When the mythos phase ends: Each investigator at Silenus's location must decide (choose one) - - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - Remove each card in your hand from the game. - Take 3 horror.

name: The Inescapable Maw: Servant of Silenus
text: Hunter.  Anytime an investigator is forced to "decide" by an ability on a blue encounter card, they must choose two options instead of one.

name: Hune-Stitched Herald
text: Warring. Forced - After Hune-Stitched Herald engages you: You must decide (choose one) - - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - Remove 2 clues from around Hub Dimension. - Hune-Stitched Herald attacks you.

name: Ethereal Entity
text: Warring. Spawn - Athenaeum of the Empty Sky. Forced - After Ethereal Entity engages you: You must decide (choose one) - - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - Place 1 of your clues on your location. - Take 2 horror.

name: Ethereal Entity
text: Warring. Spawn - Athenaeum of the Empty Sky. Forced - After Ethereal Entity engages you: You must decide (choose one) - - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - Place 1 of your clues on your location. - Take 2 horror.

name: Inevitable End
text: Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Place 1 doom on the blue agenda. - Choose 3 cards in your hand and remove them from the game. - Remove the top 6 cards of your deck from the game. Shuffle each weakness removed this way into your deck.

name: The Egg Hatches
text: Each green enemy loses aloof, loses warring, and gains hunter. Objective - If Magh'an Ark'at is defeated, (→R4).

name: Magh'an Ark'at: The Child of Paradise
text: *Magh'an Ark'at has a global health pool of 8[per_investigator]. Hunter. Massive. Retaliate. Forced - When the mythos phase ends: Place 1 resource on each [[Mutated]] enemy at Magh'an Ark'at's location, as a mutation. Deal 1 damage to each investigator at this location for each mutation on Magh'an Ark'at.

name: Vile Broodmaster: Grotesque Abomination
text: Hunter. Retaliate. Vile Broodmaster gets +1 health for each mutation on it. Forced - When 1 or more mutations would be placed on an enemy: Place 1 additional mutation on that enemy.

name: Horrific Shoggoth
text: Retaliate. Warring. Horrific Shoggoth gets +1 damage for each mutation on it. Forced - When Horrific Shoggoth attacks: Place 1 resource on it, as a mutation.

name: Bringer of Paradise
text: Aloof. Retaliate. Warring. Spawn - Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at. Forced - At the end of the round: Place 1 resource on Bringer of Paradise, as a mutation. Then, if there are 3 or more mutations on it, remove 2 of them and place 2 doom on the green agenda.

name: Bringer of Paradise
text: Aloof. Retaliate. Warring. Spawn - Shrine of Magh'an Ark'at. Forced - At the end of the round: Place 1 resource on Bringer of Paradise, as a mutation. Then, if there are 3 or more mutations on it, remove 2 of them and place 2 doom on the green agenda.

name: Hunt Down
text: Revelation - Place 1 resource on the [[Mutated]] enemy with the highest fight, as a mutation. That enemy readies, moves (one location at a time) until it reaches your location, and engages you. Then, it attacks you. If no [[Mutated]] enemies are in play, Hunt Down gains surge.

name: Ezel-zen-rezl Emerges
text: Each red enemy loses aloof, loses warring, and gains hunter. Objective - If Ezel-zen-rezl is defeated, (→R2).

name: Ezel-zen-rezl: The Lord of Swarms
text: *Ezel-zen-rezl has a global health pool of 7 [per_investigator]. Hunter. Massive. Forced - When the mythos phase ends: Each investigator at Ezel-zen-rezl's location chooses both a card from their hand and an asset they control and places them facedown underneath an [[Insect]] enemy as swarm cards.

name: Droning Horde
text: Hunter. Swarming 6. Forced - After Droning Horde enters play: Each investigator chooses a card from their hand and places it facedown underneath an [[Insect]] enemy as a swarm card. Forced - When Droning Horde attacks: It deals either its damage or its horror, instead of both.

name: Trylogog
text: Swarming 2. Warring. Spawn - Burning Pit. Forced - When Trylogog attacks: It deals either its damage or its horror, instead of both.

name: Trylogog
text: Swarming 2. Warring. Spawn - Burning Pit. Forced - When Trylogog attacks: It deals either its damage or its horror, instead of both.

name: Transmogrify
text: Revelation - Place an asset you control facedown underneath an [[Insect]] enemy as a swarm card. If no [[Insect]] is in play, search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Trylogog and spawn it. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Cloak of the Outer Realm: Interwoven Distortion
text: Uses (4 charges).  [reaction] When a non-[[Elite]] enemy would engage you, spend 1 charge: Exhaust that enemy instead.

name: Pocket Portal: Doorway to Another World
text: Fast. Forced - After Pocket Portal enters play: Place your investigator mini-card onto Pocket Portal and immediately end your turn. While your mini-card is on Pocket Portal, you are not at any location and enemies cannot spawn engaged with you. At the start of your turn, discard Pocket Portal and move to any revealed location.

name: Dreaded End: Gift of the Void
text: Uses (3 charges).  [action] Spend 1 charge: Choose one -  - Place a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location on top of the encounter deck.  - Look at the top 5 cards of the encounter deck. Discard 2 of them and return the rest to the top, in any order.

name: Blade of Ark'at: Shellblade Tribute
text: [fast] Exhaust Blade of Ark'at and spend 1 resource: Place 1 resource on Blade of Ark'at (from the token pool). [action]: Fight. You get +1 [combat] for each resource on Blade of Ark'at. If you succeed, remove up to 3 resources from Blade of Ark'at to deal that much additional damage to the attacked enemy.

name: Enchanted Skull: Mask of the Burning Pit
text: [fast] Exhaust Enchanted Skull: Place the top card of your deck facedown underneath Enchanted Skull.  [fast]: You get +1 to this skill test for each card underneath Enchanted Skull. Then discard 1 card from underneath Enchanted Skull, at random. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Machinations Through Time
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -X. X is 1 plus the number of story cards in the victory display.  [cultist]: -1. If you are at Tindalos, you automatically fail.  [tablet]: -2 (-4 instead if a [[Scientist]] asset is at your location).  [elder_thing]: -3. If you fail, deal 1 damage or 1 horror to an [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Machinations Through Time
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -X. X is 2 plus the number of story cards in the victory display.  [cultist]: -2. If you are at Tindalos, you automatically fail.  [tablet]: -3 (-6 instead of a [[Scientist]] asset is at your location).  [elder_thing]: -4. If you fail, deal 1 damange or 1 horror to an [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Into the Void
text: Forced - When a [[Scientist]] asset would be defeated: It is abducted instead (set it aside, out of play).

name: Time Bends
text:
If the Anomalies in Spacetime story card is in play: Place 1 [per_investigator] horror on a River Docks location, as anomalies. Then, add 1 anomaly to each location with an anomaly.
If the Mob Troubles story card is in play: Flip it over. Shuffle all 3 set-aside Sheldon Gang enemies into the encounter deck. If the [[Present]] Tick-Tock Club is in play, spawn the set-aside Old Sadie Sheldon enemy at it.
If the Unspeakable Abomination story card is in play: Flip it over. Spawn the set aside Tyr'thrha enemy at Tindalos.
If the A Bitter Rivalry story card is in play: Flip it over. If you are playing the past era, spawn the set-aside Edwin Bennet enemy at the lead investigator's location.

name: Time Marches On
text: Forced - When a [[Scientist]] asset would be defeated: It is abducted instead (set it aside, out of play).

name: Imprisoned in Tindalos
text: Each remaining investigator in all eras is defeated and suffers 1 mental trauma. If the Edwin Bennet asset is in play in any era, proceed to (→R2). If the Edwin Bennet enemy is in play in any era, proceed to (→R3). Otherwise, proceed to (→R4).

name: Walking Through Time
text: Abilities on [[Scientist]] assets can be triggered by any investigator at that asset's location. [reaction] When you move to a location, if one or more [[Scientist]] assets are at your location: Move any number of them with you. Objective - Restore the timeline by completing the objective on each story card. If Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski are at non-Tindalos locations in all 3 eras, and each era has no story cards in play, advance.

name: Tindalos: Realm of Angular Time
text: Tindalos is connected to each [[Portal]] location, and vice versa.  [fast] If it is your turn: Move to a [[Portal]] location.  [action]: Test [combat] (2) or [agility] (2). If you succeed, rescue an abducted [[Scientist]] asset and put it into play at Tindalos, exhausted.

name: Tindalos: Maze of Infinite Depths
text: Tindalos is connected to each [[Portal]] location, and vice versa.  [fast]: Place 1 of your clues on Tindalos, or take control of 1 clue on a Tindalos location in any era.  [action]: Test [combat] (3) or [agility] (3). If you succeed, rescue an abducted [[Scientist]] asset and put it into play at Tindalos, exhausted.

name: A Noble Legacy (Past)
text: Setup: Place the Nikola Tesla asset at the [[Past]] River Docks. Flip this story card over.

name: A Noble Legacy (Past)
text: [action] If you, Thomas Corrigan, and Mary Zielinski are all at the [[Past]] Miskatonic University: Announce "Thomas and Mary have met." [action] If Thomas Corrigan, Mary Zielinski, and Nikola Tesla are all at the same location, spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group: Announce "Thomas and Mary are inspired by Nikola Tesla." [fast] If you are at the [[Past]] Miskatonic University, spend 2 [per_investigator] resources: Announce "funding for an observatory has begun." Objective - If all 3 abilities on this story card have been triggered, add it to the victory display (Place a resource token over each ability when it is triggered).

name: Arkham Gazette
text: [action]: Test any skill (4). If you succeed, gain 1 clue (from the token pool). [fast] Remove 1 newspaper from this location: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues (from the token pool) on this location. Look at the top 5 cards of the encounter deck, add 1 to the victory display, and put the rest on the top and/or bottom of that deck in any order.

name: O'Malley's Watch Shop
text: [action] Remove 1 time from this location: Remove 1 doom from an agenda in any era. If Thomas Corrigan is at this location, you may exhaust him to place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Present]] Tick-Tock Club, as time.

name: River Docks
text: [action] Spend 2 resources or exhaust a [[Scientist]] asset at this location: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Present]] or [[Future]] River Docks, as a shipment. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Miskatonic University
text: [action] [action] Remove 1 seed from this location: Announce "a tree seed has been planted." Gain 1 resource for each [[Scientist]] asset at this location.

name: Childhood Home
text: [action] If "Thomas and Mary have met" and Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski are at this location: Announce "Thomas and Mary have married." [action] [action] If "Corrigan Industries has been founded:" Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on Corrigan Industries, as a time capsule. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Thomas Corrigan: Physics Student
text: [reaction] When you initiate a skill test, exhaust Thomas Corrigan: If you succeed, draw 1 card.

name: Mary Zielinski: Chemistry Student
text: [action] Exhaust Mary Zielinski: Investigate. If you succeed, instead of discovering a clue at your location, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Nikola Tesla: Renowned Inventor
text: [fast] Exhaust Nikola Tesla: Parley. Test [intellect] (1). If you succeed, either gain 1 clue (from the token pool), draw 2 cards, or gain 3 resources. (Max once per game per option.)

name: A Noble Legacy (Present)
text: Setup: Place the Ezra Graves asset at the [[Present]] Arkham Advertiser. Flip this story card over.

name: A Noble Legacy (Present)
text: [action] If you are at the [[Present]] Miskatonic University and "funding for an observatory has begun:" Announce "the observatory is built." [action] If "the observatory is built," "Thomas and Mary are inspired by Nikola Tesla," and Thomas Corrigan, Mary Zielinski and Ezra Graves are at the [[Present]] Miskatonic University, spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group (4 [per_investigator] clues, as a group, instead if you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode): Announce "teleportation research has begun." [fast] If "Thomas and Mary have met" and Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski are at Ye Olde Magick Shoppe, spend 1 [per_investigator] resources: Announce "Corrigan Industries has been founded" and put the set-aside Corrigan Industries location into play in the future era. Objective - If all 3 abilities on this story card have been triggered, add it to the victory display (place a resource token over each ability when it is triggered).

name: Arkham Advertiser
text: [fast] Spend 1 resource: Ready a [[Scientist]] asset at this location. (Limit once per round.) [action] Remove 1 newspaper from this location: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues (from the token pool) on this location. Take 4 additional actions this turn.

name: Tick-Tock Club
text: [action] Remove 1 time from this location: Remove 1 doom from an agenda in any era. If Thomas Corrigan is at this location, you may exhaust him to place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Future]] Tick-Tock Club, as time.

name: River Docks
text: [fast] Remove 1 shipment from this location: Gain 4 resources or 1 clue (from the token pool). [action] Spend 3 resources or exhaust 2 [[Scientist]] assets at this location: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Future]] River Docks, as a shipment.

name: Miskatonic University
text: [action] If "a tree seed has been planted:" Gain 1 clue (from the token pool) for each [[Scientist]] asset at this location. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Ye Olde Magick Shoppe
text: [fast] If Ezra Graves is at this location, exhaust him and spend 1 clue: Defeat a non-[[Elite]] enemy in any era. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Thomas Corrigan: Preeminent Astrophysicist
text: [fast] Exhaust Thomas Corrigan: You get +2 [willpower] for this skill test.  [reaction] When you initiate a skill test, exhaust Thomas Corrigan: If you succeed, draw 1 card.

name: Mary Zielinski: Distinguished Chemist
text: [fast] Exhaust Mary Zielinski: You get +2 [intellect] for this skill test. [action] Exhaust Mary Zielinski: Investigate. If you succeed, instead of discovering a clue at your location, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Ezra Graves: Professor of the Arcane
text: [fast] During any investigator's turn, exhaust Ezra Graves and spend 1 resource: Immediately take an action as if it were your turn.  [action]: Choose an [[Ally]] asset in your discard pile and put it into play under your control (Max once per game.)

name: A Noble Legacy (Future)
text: Setup: Set the Dimensional Beam Machine asset aside, out of play. Flip this story card over.

name: A Noble Legacy (Future)
text: [action] If Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski are at Corrigan Industries: Put the set-aside Dimensional Beam Machine asset into play under your control (if you are eliminated, set it aside, out of play). [action] If "teleportation research has begun" and Thomas Corrigan, Mary Zielinski, and the Dimensional Beam Machine are at the [[Future]] Miskatonic University: Test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group (8 [per_investigator] clues, as a group, instead if you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode) and announce "Thomas and Mary have made a historic discovery." [action] If "Thomas and Mary have made a historic discovery" and Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski are at the [[Future]] Arkham Advertiser: Announce "Thomas and Mary have won a Nobel Prize." Objective - If all 3 abilities on this story card have been triggered, add it to the victory display (place a resource token over each ability when it is triggered).

name: Arkham Advertiser
text: [action]: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the Arkham Gazette or on an Arkham Advertiser in any era, as a newspaper. (Group limit twice per game.) [fast] Remove 1 newspaper from this location: Place 1 [per_investigator] clues (from the token pool) on this location. Remove 1 [skull], [cultist], [tablet], or [elder_thing] token from the chaos bag for the remainder of the game.

name: Tick-Tock Club
text: [action] If Thomas Corrigan is at this location, exhaust him and spend 1 [per_investigator] clues: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on O'Malley's Watch Shop, as time. (Group limit once per game.) [action] Remove 1 time from this location: Remove 1 doom from an agenda in any era.

name: River Docks
text: [fast] Remove 1 shipment from this location: Gain 4 resources or 1 clue (from the token pool). [fast] Spend 3 resources or exhaust 2 [[Scientist]] assets at this location: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Present]] River Docks, as a shipment. (Limit once per round.)

name: Miskatonic University
text: [action] If Mary Zielinski is at this location: Place 1 resource (from the token pool) on the [[Past]] Miskatonic University, as a seed. (Group limit once per game.) [fast]: If "a tree seed has been planted:" Gain 1 clue (from the token pool) for each [[Scientist]] asset at this location. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Corrigan Industries
text: [action] Remove 1 time capsule from this location: Choose up to 4 cards in your discard pile and add them to your hand.  [action] [action] If "Thomas and Mary have married:" Their children inherit Corrigan Industries. Gain 10 resources. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Thomas Corrigan: Cofounder of Corrigan Industries
text: Each investigator at Thomas Corrigan's location gets +1 [willpower].  [reaction] When you initiate a skill test, exhaust Thomas Corrigan: If you succeed, draw 1 card.

name: Mary Zielinski: Cofounder of Corrigan Industries
text: Each investigator at Mary Zielinski's location gets +1 [intellect]. [action] Exhaust Mary Zielinski: Investigate. If you succeed, instead of discovering a clue at your location, gain 1 clue (from the token pool).

name: Dimensional Beam Machine
text: Uses (2 charges).  [fast] During your turn, exhaust Dimensional Beam Machine and spend 1 charge: Move an investigator or a non-[[Elite]] enemy at your location to any revealed location.

name: A Bitter Rivalry
text:
Setup: Follow the instructions for your relevant era(s), below.
Past: Set Thoman Corrigan aside, out of play; he has been abducted. Place Mary Zielinski at Arkham Gazette. Set the Edwin Bennet enemy aside, out of play.
Present and Future: Set Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski aside, out of play; they have been abducted. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, set the Edwin Bennet enemy aside, out of play.

name: A Bitter Rivalry
text: [action] [action]: Ready and move Edwin Bennet to your location (even if he is in another era). [action] If Edwin Bennet is at your location: Fight/Evade. Either attack or attempt to evade Edwin Bennet. If you succeed, spend X clues as a group, where X is the global number of players. Then, place 1 resource on him, as a target. (Group limit once per round.) Objective - If Edwin Bennet has 3 targets on him and he is at Tindalos, the forces of Tindalos seize him. Remove Edwin Bennet from the game and add all copies of this story card in each era to the victory display.

name: Redeem a Former Colleague
text:
Setup: Follow the instructions for your relevant era(s), below. Then, flip this story card over.
Past: Set Mary Zielinski aside, out of play; she has been abducted. Place Thomas Corrigan at Childhood Home. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, set the Edwin Bennet enemy aside, out of play.
Present: Set Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinsky aside, out of play: they have been abducted. Spawn the Edwin Bennet enemy at Miskatonic University.
Future: Set Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski aside, out of play; they have been abducted. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, set the Edwin Bennet enemy aside, out of play.

name: Redeem a Former Colleague
text: [action] [action]: Ready and move Edwin Bennet to your location (even if he is in another era). [action] If Edwin Bennet is at your location: Parley. Test [willpower] (3) or [intellect] (3). If you succeed, spend X clues, as a group where X is the global number of players. Then, place 1 resource on him, as a redemption. Objective - If Edwin Bennet has 3 or more redemption on him and he is at any Miskatonic University with Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinski, he is redeemed. Remove all redemption from him and flip him over. Add all copies of this story card in each era to the victory display.

name: Uneasy Alliance
text:
Setup: Follow the instructions for your relevant era(s), below. Then, flip this story card over.
Past: Place Thomas Corrigan at Childhood Home. Place Mary Zielinski at O'Malley's Watch Shop. Place the Edwin Bennet asset at Arkham Gazette with 12 clues on him, then remove 3 [per_investigator] clues from him.
Present and Future: Set Thomas Corrigan and Mary Zielinsky aside, out of play: they have been abducted. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, set the Edwin Bennet asset aside, out of play.

name: Uneasy Alliance
text: [action] If Edwin Bennet is at your location and he is ready: Parley. Place 1 of your clues on him. You may test [intellect] (4). If you succeed, place 1 more of your clues on him. If you fail, exhaust him. [action]: Ready and move Edwin Bennet to your loaction (even if he is in another era). Forced - At the end of the round: Remove 1 clue from Edwin Bennet (2 clues instead if you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode). If you cannot, place 1 doom on the current agenda. This effect can cause the current agenda to advance. Forced - When Edwin Bennet would be abducted: Remove all damage and horror from him and place 2 doom on the current agenda instead. Objective - If "Thomas and Mary have won a Nobel Prize" and there is no [[Plot]] story card in play in any era, add this story card to the victory display.

name: Edwin Bennet: Bitter Adversary
text: Patrol (nearest location with a [[Scientist]] asset). Edwin Bennet cannot be damaged. Clues cannot be discovered from Edwin Bennet's location. Forced - At the end of the mythos phase: Deal 1 damage to a [[Scientist]] asset at Edwin Bennet's location.

name: Edwin Bennet: Astute Associate
text: [fast] If it is your turn and you are at a [[Portal]] location, exhaust Edwin Bennet and spend 2 resources: Move to Tindalos.

name: Edwin Bennet: Envious Rival
text: Patrol (nearest location with a [[Scientist]] asset). Edwin Bennet cannot be damaged. Clues cannot be discovered from Edwin Bennet's location. Forced - At the end of the mythos phase: Deal 1 damage to each [[Scientist]] asset at Edwin Bennet's location. Then, choose an investigator to draw the top card of the encounter deck.

name: Edwin Bennet: Esteemed Colleague
text: Forced - After Edwin Bennet exhausts: Choose an investigator to draw the top card of the encounter deck. [reaction] When you draw an encounter card, exhaust Edwin Bennet: Cancel all of that card's effects and discard it.

name: Anomalies in Spacetime
text: Setup: Place 1 [per_investigator] horror on each Miskatonic University location, as anomalies. If you are playing in Epic Multiplayer Mode, also place 1 [per_investigator] horror on the following locations, as anomalies: Arkham Gazette, Arkham Advertiser, O'Malley's Watch Shop, and Tick-Tock Club. Remove the following enemies from the game: Tyr'thrha, Old Sadie Sheldon, and all 3 copies of Sheldon Gang. Flip this story card over.

name: Anomalies in Spacetime
text: Abilities on locations with an anomaly cannot be triggered. [action] Spend any number of clues: Test [willpower] (3) or [agility] (3). You get +3 skill value to this test for each clue spent this way. If you succeed, remove 1 anomaly from your location (2 anomalies instead if you succeed by 3 or more). Then, if you succeed by 4 or more and "Corrigan Industries has been founded," remove 1 anomaly from a location with the same title as your location, in any era. Objective - If it is agenda 2a and each location has no anomalies, add this story card to the victory display.

name: Mob Troubles
text: Setup: Set the Old Sadie Sheldon enemy and all 3 copies of the Sheldon Gang enemy aside, out of play. Remove the Tyr'thrha enemy from the game. You make a deal with Old Sadie Sheldon: each investigator playing the present era gains 2 resources.

name: Mob Troubles
text: [action] If "the debt has been paid," spend 1 [per_investigator] clues, as a group: Search the play area, encounter deck, or encounter discard pile for a Sheldon Gang enemy and add it to the victory display. If you searched the encounter deck, shuffle it. This action does not cause attacks of opportunity. Objective - If all 3 Sheldon Gang enemies are in the victory display, add this story card to the victory display.

name: Old Sadie Sheldon: Bootlegging Kingpin
text: Aloof. Each Sheldon Gang enemy gets +1 fight and +1 evade. [action]: Parley. Test [intellect] (2). If you succeed, spend 3 [per_investigator] resources, as a group, to add Old Sadie Sheldon to the victory display and announce "the debt has been paid."

name: Sheldon Gang
text: Surge. Hunter. Massive. Sheldon Gang gets +2 [per_investigator] health. Forced - After an investigator at Sheldon Gang's location plays a card: Ready Sheldon Gang and discard the top 3 cards of the encounter deck. Put each [[Criminal]] enemy discarded this way into play at that location.

name: Unspeakable Abomination
text: Setup: Set the Tyr'thrha enemy aside, out of play. Remove the Old Sadie Sheldon enemy and all 3 copies of the Sheldon Gang enemy from the game.

name: Unspeakable Abomination
text: [action] If Tyr'thrha is at your location, spend 1 clue: Test [intellect] (3). If you succeed, deal 2 damage to Tyr'thrha (3 damage instead if you succeed by 3 or more). This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Objective - If Tyr'thrha is defeated, add this story card to the victory display.

name: Tyr'thrha: Arch-Lord of Tindalos
text: Massive. Retaliate. Patrol (nearest location with a [[Scientist]] asset). *Tyr'thrha has a global health pool of 6 [per_investigator]. Forced - When Tyr'thrha enters a location: Each [[Scientist]] asset at that location is abducted.

name: Ghastly Satyr
text: Patrol (nearest location with a [[Scientist]] asset). Ghastly Satyr gets +1 fight while it is at a [[Past]] location and +2 fight while it is at a [[Present]] location. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Ghastly Satyr is ready: Deal 1 damage to each [[Scientist]] asset at its location.

name: Hound of Tindalos
text: Patrol (nearest location with a [[Scientist]] asset). Hound of Tindalos gets +1 health while it is at a [[Present]] location and +2 health while it is at a [[Future]] location. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase, if Hound of Tindalos is ready: Deal 1 horror to each [[Scientist]] asset at its location, and move each [[Scientist]] asset and investigator at its location to Tindalos.

name: Many-Angled Thing
text: Spawn - Tindalos. Hunter. While Many-Angled Thing is at a... - ...[[Past]] location, it gets +1 evade and gains alert. - ...[[Present]] location, it gets +1 damage and +1 horror. - ...[[Future]] location, it gets +1 fight and gains retaliate.

name: Tindalos Alpha
text: Alert. Retaliate. Forced - After Tindalos Alpha attacks you: Shuffle a non-story asset you control into your deck, moving all damage and horror from that asset to your investigator. Discard Tindalos Alpha. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Discard Tindalos Alpha.

name: A Tear in Time
text: Revelation - Test [willpower] (3). For each point you fail by, you must either lose 1 action or take 1 horror.

name: Abducted
text: Peril. Revelation - If there are no [[Ally]] assets at your location, Abducted gains surge. Otherwise, test [willpower] (5). If you fail, exhaust all [[Ally]] assets at your location and deal 5 horror to one of them.

name: Broken Space
text: Revelation - If you are at a... - ...[[Past]] location, lose 3 resources. - ...[[Present]] location, place 2 of your clues on it. - ...[[Future]] location, lose 1 action.

name: Dimensional Breach
text: Revelation - Attach to the nearest [[Portal]] location. Place 1 doom on Dimensional Breach. Each enemy at attached location gets +1 fight, +1 evade, and +1 health. [action]: Test [willpower] (3). If you succeed, discard Dimensional Breach.

name: From All Angles
text: Revelation - Test [agility] (4). If you fail and you are at a... - ...[[Past]] location, exhaust all assets at it. - ...[[Present]] location, take 1 direct damage. Deal 1 damage to each [[Ally]] asset at your location. - ...[[Future]] location, take 1 direct horror. Deal 1 horror to each [[Ally]] asset at your location.

name: Lost in Time
text: Revelation - Shuffle a non-story asset you control into your deck, moving all damage and horror from that asset to your investigator. If no asset is shuffled into your deck by this effect, choose and discard 3 cards from your hand.

name: Merging Timelines
text: Revelation - Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. For each card in your hand that matches one of the discarded cards (by title), discard that card from your hand and lose 1 action. Shuffle each weakness discarded by this effect into your deck.

name: Open Portal
text: Revelation - Take 1 damage. Test [agility] (3). If you fail, take 1 additional damage and move to Tindalos.

name: Temporal Distortion
text: Revelation - Attach to the current agenda. Abilities on story cards cannot be triggered. [action]: If you are at a [[Past]] location, you may test [willpower] (2). If you are at a [[Present]] location, you may test [agility] (3). If you are at a [[Future]] location, you may test [combat] (4). If you succeed at 1 or more of these tests, discard Temporal Distortion.

name: Vanishing History
text: Revelation - You must decide (choose one): - Discard a non-story [[Item]] asset you control. - Choose and discard 3 non-weakness cards from your hand.

name: Fortune and Folly
text: Easy / Standard  [skull]: -X. X is half your alarm level (rounded up).  [cultist]: Reveal another token. If you fail, raise your alarm level by 1.  [tablet]: -3. If you fail, the nearest enemy moves once toward you.  [elder_thing]: -5. You may raise your alarm level by 1 to automatically succeed, instead.

name: Fortune and Folly
text: Hard / Expert  [skull]: -X. X is your alarm level.  [cultist]: Reveal another token. If you fail, raise your alarm level by 1.  [tablet]: -4. The nearest enemy moves once toward you.  [elder_thing]: -6. You may raise your alarm level by 1 to automatically succeed, instead.

name: The House Always Watches
text: Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Each investigator who is at the same location as a ready [[Casino]] enemy raises their alarm level by 1. Forced - After an investigator defeats a [[Casino]] enemy: Raise that investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: Worn Out Welcome
text: If the investigators, as a group, possess 10 [per_investigator] or more resources, remember that you "cleaned out the house." (→R1)

name: Opening Hand
text: Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Each investigator who is at the same location as a ready [[Casino]] enemy raises their alarm level by 1. Forced - After an investigator defeats a [[Casino]] enemy: Raise that investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: Bluff Called
text: Spawn the set-aside Dimensional Shambler at the Roulette Wheel. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the set-aside Plan in Shambles encounter set into the encounter deck. Raise each investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: The Turn
text: Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Each investigator who is at the same location as a ready [[Casino]] enemy raises their alarm level by 1. Forced - After an investigator defeats a [[Casino]] enemy: Raise that investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: Improbable Chaos
text: Shuffle the 3 set-aside Fortune's Disfavor story cards and select 1 of them at random, resolving its text.  Raise each investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: All Bets Down
text: Forced - At the start of the enemy phase: Each investigator who is at the same location as a ready [[Casino]] enemy raises their alarm level by 1. Forced - After an investigator defeats a [[Casino]] enemy: Raise that investigator's alarm level by 1.

name: Cleaned Out
text: Each investigator who did not resign is defeated and suffers 1 physical trauma. (→R3)

name: Casing the Joint
text: [reaction] After you trigger the [action] ability on a [[Game]] location: Remove 1 clue from The Wellspring of Fortune (2 clues instead if you gained resources from the [[Game]] location's ability). Objective - Accomplish as many tasks as you can and get out before you are noticed! If each undefeated investigator has resigned, advance.

name: Come Back Soon!
text: Each investigator who was defeated raises their alarm level by 2. If the investigators, as a group, possess 10 [per_investigator] or more resources, remember that you "cleaned out the house." (→R2)

name: The Take
text: [reaction] After you trigger the [action] ability on a [[Game]] location: Remove 1 clue from The Wellspring of Fortune (2 clues instead if you gained resources from the [[Game]] location's ability). Objective - If an investigator controls The Wellspring of Fortune, advance.

name: Relic Acquired
text:
If Abarran Arrigorriagakoa is still in play...
Just as you are ready to celebrate victory, a voice calls from the door behind you. "You come into my casino and steal from me?" Abarran stands there, shaking with fury. "You'll ruin everything I built. But I won't let you!" Any front of professionalism has been dropped. The true danger of the man has been revealed.
Move Abarran Arrigorriagakoa (Abarran Unleashed) to Relic Room.
If Abarran Arrigorriagakoa is in the victory display...
A ragged voice gasps from behind you. "I won't let you take it!" Abarran is somehow still on his feet in the doorway behind you. Although he looks ready to collapse, his eyes burn with hatred. Before you can stop him, he slams his fist against some kind of mechanism on the wall before slumping to the ground. Large sections of the solid steel ceiling begin to fall from above...
Each investigator must test [agility] (3). Each investigator who fails takes 2 damage.

name: The Exit
text: Each [[Casino]] enemy loses aloof and patrol. Each [[Casino]] enemy gains hunter and "Prey - Investigator that controls The Wellspring of Fortune." Objective - If the investigator with The Wellspring of Fortune has resigned, advance. (Beware - any investigator who has not resigned will be left behind!)

name: Sweet Freedom
text: Each investigator who has not resigned is defeated and suffers 1 physical trauma. (→R1)

name: Casino Floor: Calm Night
text: [action]: Resign. It's time to get out of here! [action]: Place an asset from your hand or play area facedown beneath Casino Floor, as part of your crew's "stash for the heist." (Limit once per turn.)

name: Casino Floor: Busy Night
text: [action]: Resign. It's time to get out of here! [action] If an investigator "impersonated a high roller" and your alarm level is 6 or lower, spend 2 resources: You set up a delivery to the vault. Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a [[Casino]] enemy, spawn it here, and attach the set-aside Package Delivery story card to it. (Group limit once per game.)

name: Poker Table
text: [action] Spend 2 resources: Discard the top 5 cards of the encounter deck and check their game icons. You may mulligan once. If all 5 cards have a sequential rank, or if 3 or more of the cards have the same rank, gain 5 resources.

name: Roulette Wheel
text: [action] Spend 1 resource: Name a color and a rank. Then, discard the top card of the encounter deck and check its game icon. If it matches the named color and rank, gain 5 resources.

name: Baccarat Table
text: [action] Spend 2 resources: Name "player," "banker," or "tie." Discard the top 2 cards of the encounter deck and check their game icons. If the combined value of their ranks is higher than 18, the "player" wins. If the combined value is lower than 18, the "banker" wins. If the combined value is equal to 18, it is a "tie." If this result matches the result you named, gain 4 resources.

name: Slot Machines
text: [action] Spend 1 resource: Discard the top 3 cards of the encounter deck and check their game icons. If all 3 cards have the same suit or same rank, gain 3 resources.

name: High Roller's Table: Calm Night
text: [action] If there are no clues here, spend 4 resources: Remember that you "impersonated a high roller." Discard the top 5 cards of the encounter deck and check their game icons. You may mulligan once. If all 5 cards have the same suit or a sequential rank, or if 4 of the cards have the same rank, gain 8 resources.

name: High Roller's Table: Busy Night
text: [action] Spend 4 resources: Discard the top 5 cards of the encounter deck and check their game icons. You may mulligan once. If all 5 cards have the same suit or a sequential rank, or if 4 cards have the same rank, gain 8 resources and remember that you "won a cultist medallion."

name: Casino Lounge: Calm Night
text: [action] If there are no clues on Casino Lounge: Test [agility] (3). If you succeed, remember that you "found a vent."

name: Casino Lounge: Busy Night
text: [action] If you "found a vent," investigators at Casino Lounge spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group: Move. Move to Guard Room. Then, reveal Staff Access Hallway if it has not already been revealed. Remember that "the vent is open." (Group limit once per game.) While "the vent is open," investigators may move from Casino Lounge to Guard Room as if they were connected, and vice versa.

name: Staff Access Hallway
text: Forced - When Staff Access Hallway is revealed: Spawn Abarran Arrigorriagakoa (Abarran Unleashed) at Owner's Office. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the set-aside Fortune's Chosen encounter set into the encounter deck.

name: Staff Access Hallway
text: As an additional cost to enter Staff Access Hallway, investigators at a connecting location must spend 4 [per_investigator] clues, as a group.

name: Security Office
text: [action]: Choose 1, 2, or 3. Discard that many cards from the top of the encounter deck and check their game icons. If an icon matches the rank of an enemy in play, remember that you "observed the staff." If the combined value of the discarded cards is 19 or higher, draw the top card in the encounter discard pile.

name: Guard Room
text: [action] If all of the following are true, remember that you "impersonated a guard:"  - There are no enemies at Guard Room.  - There are no clues remaining on Guard Room.  - An investigator "obtained an employee uniform."

name: Owner's Office
text: X is equal to the highest alarm level among investigators at Owner's Office.  [action] If there are no clues on Owner's Office: Remember that you "found Abarran's sigil."

name: Owner's Office
text: The Owner's Office is locked. As an additional cost to enter Owner's Office, investigators at a connecting location must spend 2 [per_investigator] clues, as a group, unless an investigator "stole Abarran's keys."

name: Counting Room
text: [action] Investigators at Counting Room spend 10 [per_investigator] resources, as a group (5 [per_investigator] instead if you "found a vent"): Remember that you "obtained a schematic."  [reaction] After you successfully investigate Counting Room by 2 or more: Gain 2 resources. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Vault Door
text: [action] Move. Discard the top card of the encounter deck and check its game icon (if an investigator "obtained a schematic," you may mulligan up to two times): - Ifits suit is ♣ or ♠: Move to Guard Room. - If its suit is ♥: Move to Casino Lounge. - If its suit is ♦: Move to Baccarat Table.

name: Vault Door
text: The door to the casino vault is locked, guarded, and warded with magic. You cannot enter Vault Door unless you have completed at least 4 of the tasks on The Heist story card.

name: Relic Room: Sanctum of Fortune
text: Forced - After Relic Room is revealed: Move all clues from The Wellspring of Fortune to Relic Room. [action] If there are no clues on Relic Room: Take control of The Wellspring of Fortune and attach it to your investigator card.

name: Relic Room: Sanctum of Fortune
text: You cannot enter Relic Room.  Vault Door gains: "[action] Take 1 damage and 1 horror unless an investigator "found Abarran's sigil": Test [willpower] or [agility] (4). If you succeed, investigators at Vault Door may spend 4 [per_investigator] clues, as a group, to reveal Relic Room.

name: The Stakeout
text:
Each of the following is a task that can be completed in Fortune and Folly, Part I: □ An investigator "found a vent." □ An investigator "impersonated a high roller." □ An investigator "convinced Isamara to participate in the heist." □ An investigator "obtained an employee uniform." □ An investigator "stole Abarran's keys." □ An investigator "cleaned out the house."
Forced - When an investigator is eliminated (by defeat or resignation): Place each of that investigator's clues on this story card, instead of placing them at their location.

name: The Heist
text: Each of the following is a task that can be completed in Fortune and Folly, Part II: □ An investigator "won a cultist medallion." □ An investigator "observed the staff." □ An investigator "found Abarran's sigil." □ An investigator "obtained a schematic." □ An investigator "impersonated a guard." □ An investigator "stayed out of sight." □ An investigator "delivered a decoy package." □ "Isamara mesmerized the guards with her song." Hint: You must complete at least 4 of these tasks to unlock the vault door.

name: If the Uniform Fits...
text:
This casino employee looks like they are wearing a uniform in your size.
[action]: Fight. Perform this attack only on the attached enemy. Test [combat] (3). If you succeed, attached enemy has been knocked out (read the section below). If you fail, raise your alarm level by 1. If attached enemy leaves play, remove this story card from the game.
If attached enemy is knocked out: Remember that you "obtained an employee uniform." Raise your alarm level by 1 if another enemy is at your location or a connecting location. Discard the attached enemy. Remove this story card from the game.

name: Package Delivery
text:
You pass the package to the employee.
The attached enemy's patrol location changes to Vault Door. Attached enemy loses the [[Casino]] trait. If attached enemy enters Vault Door, the package has been delivered (read the section below). If attached enemy leaves play, remove this story card from the game.
If the package has been delivered: Choose an investigator to search their deck for an [[Ally]] or [[Item]] asset and place it facedown beneath Vault Door. Remember that you "delivered a decoy package." Discard the attached enemy and attach this story card to Vault Door. [action]: Put the facedown asset beneath Vault Door into play under your control. Remove this story card from the game.

name: Fortune's Disfavor
text:
Isamara warned you that the relic you seek possesses strange abilities that bring misfortune upon those who are not favored by its bearer. Perhaps, then, you should have factored a sudden turn of fortune into your plans. For, at this moment, in the midst of your high-stakes heist, everything is about to be turned upside-down...
Flip this card over and resolve the text on its other side.

name: Lights Out!
text:
Just as you are about to act, there is a sudden pop and a spark, and the casino plunges into total darkness. There are panicked whispers from all around. This seems like an unfortunately timed malfunction of some kind. Now you must find your way forward in the dark unless you can find a way to restore the power.
Put this card into play next to the agenda deck. Place a damage token on each location. Each location with a damage token has no lights and gains: "This location gets +1 shroud. As an additional cost to trigger an ability on this location, you must either spend an additional action or take 1 horror." While an investigator is at Security Office, they may perform the following action: [action]: Spend 1 [per_investigator] clues. Choose one - - Restore power to the main hall. Remove a damage token from each [[Public]] location. - Restore power to the staff area. Remove a damage token from each [[Restricted]] location.

name: Fortune's Disfavor
text:
Isamara warned you that the relic you seek possesses strange abilities that bring misfortune upon those who are not favored by its bearer. Perhaps, then, you should have factored a sudden turn of fortune into your plans. For, at this moment, in the midst of your high-stakes heist, everything is about to be turned upside-down...

name: Unlikely Injuries
text:
What suddenly strikes the casino can only be described as a series of extremely unlikely and unfortunate mishaps. Glasses shatter, stray dice cause patrons to slip and fall, and chandeliers crash onto skulls.
Put this card into play next to the agenda deck. One at a time, each investigator discards the top card of the encounter deck and checks its game icon. The following injury applies to that investigator for the remainder of the game: - If its suit is ♥: You are limited to one move action per turn. - If its suit is ♦: You are limited to one investigate action per turn. - If its suit is ♣: You are limited to one fight action per turn. - If its suit is ♠: You are limited to one activate action per turn. [fast] Take 1 damage or 1 horror: Ignore the effects of your injury until the end of your turn. Any investigator may trigger this ability. (Limit once per turn.)

name: Personal Entanglement
text:
You have learned that someone from your past works in this casino - what are the odds? Unfortunately, Abarran has learned of this connection as well. You have to rescue them before it's too late!
Attach this story card to Owner's Office. This story card represents somebody connected to the lead investigator. [action] Investigators at Owner's Office spend 1 [per_investigator] clues, as a group: Remove this story card from the game. Forced - At the end of the enemy phase: Place 1 horror on this story card (2 horror instead if there is an enemy at Owner's Office).
If there is 6 or more horror on this story card: You are too late. The lead investigator suffers 1 mental trauma. Each investigator takes 2 direct horror. Remove this story card from the game.

name: Subject 5U-21: The Anomaly
packName: The Blob That Ate Everything ELSE!
text: Deck Size: 50. Deckbuilding Options: Neutral cards level 0-5, cards from any class level 0. Deckbuilding Requirements (do not count toward deck size): Ravenous, 3 copies of Regurgitation, 3 copies of Reality Acid, 2 random basic weaknesses. Deckbuilding Restrictions: No permanents except story and signature permanents. Additional Requirements: Your deck must include at least 7 cards from each class ([guardian], [seeker], [rogue], [mystic], and [survivor]). Additional Upgrade Options: After each scenario ends, separate each card you devoured (beneath Ravenous and set-aside) by class. Before the next scenario in this campaign, you may spend experience to purchase or upgrade 1 or more cards of any level from the class with the most devoured cards (if there is a tie, choose one).

name: Ravenous: Controlled Hunger
packName: The Blob That Ate Everything ELSE!
text: Subject 5U-21 deck only. Permanent. Each time you devour a card, place it facedown beneath Ravenous. You get +1 to each of your skills for each card beneath Ravenous (max +5). Forced - If there are 5 or more cards beneath Ravenous: Flip it over.

name: Ravenous: Uncontrolled Hunger

text: Subject 5U-21 deck only. Permanent. Each time you devour a card, set it aside, out of play. You get +1 to each of your skills for each card beneath Ravenous (max +5). Forced - At the end of your turn: Devour a non-story card beneath Ravenous or controlled by an investigator at your location. If there is nothing beneath Ravenous, flip it over.

name: Regurgitation
